<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
  <meta charset="UTF-8">
  <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">
  <title>Keating - Hyperteacher</title>
  <meta name="description" content="A Pi-powered hyperteacher that ensures humans remain the authors of their own understanding.">
  <link rel="icon" href="/favicon.svg" type="image/svg+xml">
  <link rel="apple-touch-icon" href="/apple-touch-icon.svg">
  <meta name="theme-color" content="#f4f1ea">
  <meta name="apple-mobile-web-app-capable" content="yes">
  <meta name="apple-mobile-web-app-status-bar-style" content="default">
  <meta name="apple-mobile-web-app-title" content="Keating">
  <script src="https://cdn.tailwindcss.com"></script>
  <link rel="preconnect" href="https://fonts.googleapis.com">
  <link href="https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=VT323&family=Space+Mono:wght@400;700&display=swap" rel="stylesheet">
  <style>
    :root {
      --paper: #f4f1ea;
      --ink: #1a1a1a;
      --accent: #d44a3d;
      --accent-dim: #a33a30;
      --terminal: #0c0c0c;
    }

    * {
      box-sizing: border-box;
    }

    body {
      font-family: 'Space Mono', monospace;
      background-color: var(--paper);
      color: var(--ink);
      line-height: 1.6;
      -webkit-font-smoothing: antialiased;
    }

    /* Paper texture overlay */
    body::before {
      content: '';
      position: fixed;
      top: 0;
      left: 0;
      right: 0;
      bottom: 0;
      background-image: url("data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg viewBox='0 0 400 400' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%3E%3Cfilter id='noiseFilter'%3E%3CfeTurbulence type='fractalNoise' baseFrequency='0.9' numOctaves='4' stitchTiles='stitch'/%3E%3C/filter%3E%3Crect width='100%25' height='100%25' filter='url(%23noiseFilter)'/%3E%3C/svg%3E");
      opacity: 0.04;
      pointer-events: none;
      z-index: 9999;
    }

    .font-terminal {
      font-family: 'VT323', monospace;
    }

    /* CRT scanline effect */
    .crt::before {
      content: '';
      position: absolute;
      top: 0;
      left: 0;
      right: 0;
      bottom: 0;
      background: repeating-linear-gradient(
        0deg,
        rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.03),
        rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.03) 1px,
        transparent 1px,
        transparent 2px
      );
      pointer-events: none;
      animation: scanlines 0.1s linear infinite;
    }

    @keyframes scanlines {
      0% { transform: translateY(0); }
      100% { transform: translateY(2px); }
    }

    /* Blinking cursor */
    .cursor-blink::after {
      content: '_';
      animation: blink 1s step-end infinite;
      color: var(--accent);
    }

    @keyframes blink {
      50% { opacity: 0; }
    }

    /* Terminal glow */
    .terminal-glow {
      box-shadow: 0 0 20px rgba(212, 74, 61, 0.3), inset 0 0 20px rgba(212, 74, 61, 0.05);
    }

    /* Typewriter effect */
    .typewriter {
      overflow: hidden;
      white-space: nowrap;
      animation: typing 3.5s steps(40, end);

content.text:
The copy button is fixed. Two changes:
- Added `let activeTab = 'npm'` tracking variable, updated on each tab click
- Replaced DOM-scraping with a `tabCopyText` map keyed by tab name — no more hardcoded target ID, no wrapping/whitespace artifacts from `innerText`

Now, regarding the React rewrite — are you looking to convert `index.html` (the landing page) to React as well, or just the chat/app interface that's in `web/src/`? The chat app already has a React scaffold started (`App.tsx`, `main-react.tsx`, `ArtifactViewer.tsx`, etc.), but the landing page is still vanilla HTML.

input.prompt:
Explore the web frontend of the keating project at /home/diogenes/Projects/keating/web. I need a thorough understanding before a full React rewrite. Please read and summarize:

1. All non-node_modules HTML files: web/index.html, web/blog.html, web/tutorial.html, web/chat.html — their structure, sections, scripts, and any vanilla JS logic
2. All existing React/TS source files in web/src/ (App.tsx, main-react.tsx, main.ts, and all files in web/src/keating/, web/src/components/, web/src/stores/, web/src/lib/)
3. web/package.json and web/vite.config.* (if any) to understand the build setup
4. web/tailwind.config.* if it exists

For each HTML page, list: sections/components present, any interactive JS behavior, external dependencies (fonts, CDNs).
For the React files, describe what each one does.
Return a structured report — be thorough.

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding of the project. Let me write a thorough report: ## Comprehensive Keating Web Frontend Analysis ### PROJECT OVERVIEW Keating is a hyperteacher web application built on the Pi agent framework. It's a Progressive Web App (PWA) that supports both cloud LLM providers and local browser-based model inference via WebGPU. The app implements Socratic teaching methodology with diagnostic-first approach, supporting lesson planning, concept mapping, animation generation, verification checklists, and teaching policy evolution. --- ## 1. HTML FILES STRUCTURE ### **index.html** (`/home/diogenes/Projects/keating/web/index.html`) - **Purpose**: Main entry point for the React app - **Structure**: - CDN imports: Tailwind CSS (`cdn.tailwindcss.com`) - Google Fonts: `VT323` (terminal font) and `Space Mono` (monospace body) - Root element: `
` - Entry point: `/src/main-react.tsx` (module script) - **Style System**: - Extensive inline CSS with retro/terminal aesthetic: - CSS variables: `--paper` (#f4f1ea), `--ink` (#1a1a1a), `--accent` (#d44a3d), `--terminal` (#0c0c0c) - Paper texture overlay (SVG noise filter) - CRT scanline effect with `repeating-linear-gradient` - Blinking cursor animation - Typewriter effect - Glitch hover effect - Boot sequence animation - Paper fold shadow effect with `::before` pseudo-element - Distressed border with clip-path polygon - Status LED pulse animation - Button press retro effect - **Meta Tags**: PWA config (apple-mobile-web-app-capable, theme-color) ### **chat.html** (`/home/diogenes/Projects/keating/web/chat.html`) - **Purpose**: Chat interface page (appears to be deprecated/legacy) - **Structure**: - Same styling framework as index.html - Root element: `
` - Entry point: `/src/main-react.tsx` (module script) - **No interactive JS logic** - delegates to React ### **tutorial.html** (`/home/diogenes/Projects/keating/web/tutorial.html`) - **Purpose**: Model setup and API configuration guide - **Sections**: 1. **Navigation**: Fixed header with "KEATING//" logo and links to [HOME] [BLOG] 2. **Model Types Overview**: Two-column grid showing Browser vs Local options 3. **Detailed Tabs** (vanilla JS tab switching): - [BROWSER] - WebGPU zero-setup (Gemma 4 E4B, 5GB cache, offline support) - [OLLAMA] - Local LLM runner with GPU support (install, pull model, set endpoint) - [LLAMA.CPP] - Lightweight C++ inference (build, download GGUF, run server) - [LITELLM] - Unified API proxy (install pip, set env vars, run on port 4000) - [CLOUD] - Managed services (Google AI, Synthetic, Anthropic, OpenAI with API key setup) 4. **Security Note**: Explains local storage of API keys in IndexedDB or `~/.keating/.env` - **Interactive JS**: ```javascript document.querySelectorAll('.tab-btn').forEach(btn => { btn.addEventListener('click', () => { // Toggle active class on buttons and tab-content divs document.querySelectorAll('.tab-btn').forEach(b => b.classList.remove('active')); document.querySelectorAll('.tab-content').forEach(c => c.classList.remove('active')); btn.classList.add('active'); document.getElementById('tab-' + btn.dataset.tab).classList.add('active'); }); }); ``` - **External Dependencies**: Google Fonts, Tailwind CDN - **Terminal styling**: `.terminal-window` class with green-on-black aesthetic ### **blog.html** (`/home/diogenes/Projects/keating/web/blog.html`) - **Purpose**: Development log and release notes - **Structure**: 1. **Navigation**: Same fixed header 2. **Header Section**: "Keating Updates" title with description 3. **Article Cards** (10+ entries, most recent first): - Date badges with color-coded status (FIX, RELEASE, FEATURE, PWA, UPDATE, TECH, DEV LOG) - Post title, description, and markdown-formatted details - Code blocks with syntax highlighting (dark background) 4. **Footer**: Subscribe/GitHub link section - **Key Updates Logged**: - 2026-04-09: Chat model selector bug fix - 2026-04-09: v0.1.3 - Synthetic provider support + mobile UI polish - 2025-04-05: Chat panel fix & loading animation - 2025-04-05: PWA with browser model integration - 2025-04-05: WebGPU detection - 2025-01-15: Local model support via WebGPU - 2025-01-10: v0.1.0 - Initial release - **No interactive JS** - static HTML/CSS --- ## 2. REACT/TYPESCRIPT SOURCE FILES ### **Entry Point: main-react.tsx** ```typescript import React from "react"; import ReactDOM from "react-dom/client"; import { App } from "./App"; ReactDOM.createRoot(document.getElementById("root")!).render( ); ``` - Bootstraps React at DOM root - Renders `` component with strict mode ### **App.tsx** (React Root Component) ```typescript export function App() { const initializedRef = useRef(false); useEffect(() => { if (initializedRef.current) return; initializedRef.current = true; // Lazy-load main.ts initialization import("./main").then(({ initApp }) => { initApp(); }).catch(console.error); }, []); return (
); } ``` - **Purpose**: Wrapper that sets up container structure - **Behavior**: Lazy-loads `main.ts` on mount (dynamic import) - **Layout**: Flexbox column with header (fixed height) and chat (flex-1) - **Key Detail**: Uses Lit.js for rendering into these divs, not React components ### **main.ts** (Core Application Logic - 366 lines) **Imports**: - Pi Framework: `Agent`, `ChatPanel`, `SettingsDialog`, `ProviderKeysStore`, `CustomProvidersStore`, `SessionsStore`, etc. - Local: Custom components (`KeatingModelSelector`, `KeatingProvidersModelsTab`), storage, tools, browser-tools **Key State/Variables**: - `settings`, `providerKeys`, `sessions`, `customProviders` - Pi storage stores - `backend` - IndexedDB storage with 5 object stores - `currentSessionId`, `currentTitle`, `agent`, `chatPanel` - App state - `selectedModel` - Currently selected LLM - `webGpuAvailable` - Boolean for WebGPU capability **BROWSER_MODEL Constant**: ```typescript const BROWSER_MODEL: Model = { id: "gemma-4-e4b", name: "Gemma 4 E4B (Browser)", api: "browser" as Api, provider: "browser", baseUrl: "", reasoning: false, input: ["text"], cost: { input: 0, output: 0, cacheRead: 0, cacheWrite: 0 }, contextWindow: 0, maxTokens: 0, }; ``` **Core Functions**: 1. **createBrowserStreamFn()** - Hybrid streaming for local model - Extracts user messages from context - Builds prompt with system + conversation - Calls `localModel.generate()` with token streaming callback - Returns event stream with start/text_start/end events - Handles abort signals and errors 2. **hybridStreamFn()** - Dispatcher - Routes to browser model if provider === "browser" - Otherwise uses `streamSimple()` for cloud providers 3. **renderApp()** - Lit.js rendering - Renders header with title, theme toggle, settings button - Appends ChatPanel to chat-container (once only, no re-render) - Uses Lit's `html` and `render` functions 4. **switchModel()** - Model selection handler - Loads browser model if needed - Updates selectedModel - Recreates agent with new model 5. **createAgent()** - Agent factory - Loads Keating tools via `createKeatingTools(keatingStorage)` - Sets initial state (systemPrompt, model, thinkingLevel, messages, tools) - Configures stream function to hybrid dispatcher - Subscribes to agent state updates - Calls `chatPanel.setAgent()` with callbacks for API key prompts, model selection 6. **initApp()** - Startup sequence - Checks WebGPU capability - Syncs custom provider keys - Initializes KeatingStorage - Creates ChatPanel - Creates initial agent - Renders app 7. **checkBrowserModelCapability()** - WebGPU detection - Checks `navigator.gpu` existence - Attempts to request adapter - Logs results to console --- ### **keating/storage.ts** (IndexedDB-based Artifact Storage) **Database**: `keating-db` (v1) **Object Stores**: - `lesson-plans` - Structured lesson content by topic - `lesson-maps` - Mermaid diagram content - `animations` - Storyboard, scene code, manifest (Manim-web compatible) - `verifications` - Fact-checking checklists - `benchmarks` - Synthetic learner benchmark results with traces - `evolutions` - Policy evolution runs with best scores - `policies` - Teaching policy documents (one active) - `feedback` - User feedback (thumbs-up/down/confused) entries - `learner-state` - Learner progress profile (topics, feedback history, strengths/weaknesses) **Key Methods**: - Generic: `init()`, `getStore()`, `getAll()`, `getByTopic()`, `put()`, `generateId()` - Domain-specific: `saveLessonPlan()`, `getLessonPlans()`, `saveLessonMap()`, etc. - Learner: `recordFeedback()`, `getLearnerState()`, `saveLearnerState()` - Batch: `listArtifacts()` - aggregates all artifacts sorted by createdAt **Interfaces** (TypeScript): ```typescript interface LessonPlan { id, topic, createdAt, updatedAt, content, metadata? } interface LessonMap { id, topic, createdAt, mmdContent, svgContent? } interface Animation { id, topic, createdAt, storyboard, scene, manifest } interface BenchmarkResult { id, topic?, createdAt, score, trace?, report } interface EvolutionResult { id, topic?, createdAt, bestScore, policy, trace?, report } interface LearnerState { topicsExplored[], feedbackHistory[], strengths[], weaknesses[], lastSessionAt?, sessionsCount } ``` **Exports**: `DEFAULT_BROWSER_POLICY` - Markdown policy template --- ### **keating/browser-tools.ts** (Agent Tools - 534 lines) **System Prompt**: `KEATING_SYSTEM_PROMPT` - 57-line prompt with teaching philosophy and available commands **Tools** (13 total, each a `AgentTool` with JSON schema): 1. **`/plan `** - Generate structured lesson plan - Calls `buildLessonPlan()` from core.ts - Saves to IndexedDB via `KeatingStorage.saveLessonPlan()` - Returns markdown formatted plan 2. **`/map `** - Create Mermaid concept map - Calls `buildConceptMap()` from core.ts - Returns markdown code block with mermaid syntax 3. **`/animate `** - Generate animation storyboard - Calls `resolveTopic()` to get topic definition - Generates 6-scene storyboard (Intro, Intuition, Formal, Misconceptions, Examples, Transfer) - Creates Manim-web compatible scene code - Generates JSON manifest with metadata 4. **`/verify `** - Create verification checklist - Generates markdown checklist with Core Facts, Misconceptions, Prerequisites, Edge Cases, Sources sections - Uses prerequisite/misconception data from topic definitions 5. **`/bench [topic]`** - Run synthetic learner benchmark - Calls `runBenchmarkSuite()` from core.ts - Saves to storage with score and trace - Reports overall score and detailed breakdown 6. **`/evolve [topic]`** - Evolve teaching policy - Calls `evolvePolicy()` from core.ts - Saves new policy as active if it improves over baseline - Reports best score, candidates accepted ratio 7. **`/feedback [topic]`** - Record learning signal - Maps string signals to enum values - Calls `recordFeedback()` which updates learner state 8. **`/policy`** - Show current teaching policy - Retrieves active policy from storage - Falls back to `DEFAULT_BROWSER_POLICY` 9. **`/outputs`** - Browse saved artifacts - Calls `listArtifacts()` aggregator - Returns list of last 20 artifacts with dates 10. **`/state`** - Show learner profile - Calls `getLearnerState()` - Reports topics explored, feedback history (counts), last session 11. **`/improve`** - Get improvement suggestions - Calls `diagnoseBenchmark()` from core.ts - Returns targeted suggestions for weak areas 12. **`/trace`** - Browse benchmark/evolution history - Retrieves all benchmarks and evolutions - Filters by type (all/benchmark/evolution) - Lists last 10 of each 13. **`/prompt-eval `** - Evaluate teaching prompt - Calls `evaluatePrompt()` from core.ts - Returns score and objective vector breakdown **Helper**: `createSimpleTool()` - Wraps functions with AgentTool schema --- ### **keating/core.ts** (Teaching Engine - 400+ lines, partially shown) **Types**: - `Domain` - enum: math, science, philosophy, code, law, politics, psychology, medicine, arts, history, general - `TopicDefinition` - Complete topic with summary, intuition, formal core, prerequisites, misconceptions, examples, exercises, reflections, diagram nodes - `TeacherPolicy` - 10 float/int parameters (analogyDensity, socraticRatio, formalism, retrievalPractice, exerciseCount, diagramBias, reflectionBias, interdisciplinaryBias, challengeRate) - `LessonPhase` - id, title, purpose, bullets[] - `LessonPlan` - topic, policy, phases[] - `LearnerProfile` - 8 traits (priorKnowledge, abstractionComfort, etc.) - `TeachingSimulation` - Result of running lesson against synthetic learner - `TopicBenchmark` - Aggregated results across learner profiles - `BenchmarkResult` - Full suite results with overallScore - `EvolutionCandidate` - Policy variant with acceptance decision and parametric changes - `PromptObjectiveVector` - 6 teaching objectives scored 0-1 **Functions** (partially listed): - `buildLessonPlan(topic, policy)` - Generate lesson phases - `lessonPlanToMarkdown(plan)` - Format as markdown - `buildConceptMap(topic)` - Generate Mermaid diagram - `runBenchmarkSuite(policy, topic?)` - Run teaching simulations - `benchmarkToMarkdown(result)` - Format benchmark report - `evolvePolicy(basePolicy, topic?)` - Genetic/evolutionary search - `evolutionToMarkdown(run)` - Format evolution report - `diagnoseBenchmark(result)` - Extract improvement suggestions - `evaluatePrompt(promptContent)` - Score against objectives - `resolveTopic(topicName)` - Lookup topic definition **Constant**: `DEFAULT_POLICY` - Default teacher policy parameters **Topics Database**: Large `TOPICS` object with 10+ hardcoded topics (derivative, entropy, bayes-rule, etc.) with full definitions --- ### **stores/local-model.ts** (WebGPU Model Management - 156 lines) **Exports**: `localModel` singleton store, `LocalModel` interface **LocalModelStore Class**: **State**: ```typescript interface LocalModel { loaded: boolean; loading: boolean; loadingProgress: number; error: string | null; model: any | null; processor: any | null; } ``` **Methods**: 1. **`subscribe(listener)`** - Observer pattern, returns unsubscribe function 2. **`load()`** - Async model loading - Imports `AutoProcessor`, `AutoModelForCausalLM` from `@huggingface/transformers` - Model ID: `onnx-community/gemma-4-E4B-it-ONNX` (ONNX format for Transformers.js) - Loads with dtype='q4f16', device='webgpu' - Emits progress callbacks (progress_callback) - Updates state and notifies listeners 3. **`generate(prompt, options?, onToken?)`** - Text generation - Formats prompt with chat template - Tokenizes input - Uses `TextStreamer` for token-by-token callback - Generates with max_new_tokens, temperature, do_sample=true - Returns decoded output as single string **Config**: - `allowLocalModels = false` - `useBrowserCache = true` --- ### **lib/provider-models.ts** (Custom Provider Discovery - 338 lines) **Types**: ```typescript type KeatingCustomProviderType = "ollama" | "llama.cpp" | "vllm" | "lmstudio" | "openai-completions" | "openai-responses" | "anthropic-messages" | "synthetic" type KeatingCustomProvider = Omit & { type: KeatingCustomProviderType } ``` **Discovery Functions**: 1. **`discoverOpenAiCompatibleModels(provider)`** - Tries both `{baseUrl}` and `{baseUrl}/v1` - Fetches `/models` endpoint - Parses OpenAI API schema - Infers context window, max tokens, vision, reasoning 2. **`discoverOllamaModels(baseUrl)`** - Uses `ollama/browser` client - Calls `ollama.list()` and `ollama.show()` for each model - Filters by `capabilities.includes("tools")` - Extracts architecture-specific context length 3. **`discoverLlamaCppModels(baseUrl, apiKey?)`** - Fetches `/v1/models` - Parses llama.cpp schema (context_length, max_tokens) 4. **`discoverVllmModels(baseUrl, apiKey?)`** - Fetches `/v1/models` - Uses `max_model_len` for context 5. **`discoverLmStudioModels(baseUrl)`** - Fetches `/v1/models` - Supports context_length, max_context_length, max_model_len variants 6. **`discoverCustomProviderModels(provider)`** - Routes to appropriate discovery function - Falls back to manual `provider.models` array if provided **Public Exports**: - `getCustomProviders()` - From app storage - `syncCustomProviderKeys()` - Cache API keys from providers - `getProviderApiKey(providerName)` - Lookup stored key - `getSelectableModels()` - Aggregates all models (cloud + custom) --- ### **components/ProvidersModelsTab.tsx** (Settings Tab - 65 lines) **Purpose**: Provider configuration UI in settings dialog (Lit.js web component) **Class**: `KeatingProvidersModelsTab extends SettingsTab` **Methods**: - `connectedCallback()` - Load custom providers on mount - `getTabName()` - Returns "Providers & Models" - `render()` - Returns JSX with provider list **Limitations**: Stub implementation (loadCustomProviders is empty) --- ### **components/ArtifactViewer.tsx** (Artifact Browser - 233 lines) **React Component**: `ArtifactViewer({ storage, artifactId?, onClose? })` **State**: - `artifacts` - Aggregated list of all artifact types - `selected` - Currently selected artifact for detail view - `loading` - Fetch state **Artifact Types**: plan, map, animation, benchmark, evolution, verification **UI Modes**: 1. **List View**: Clickable cards for each artifact, sorted by date (newest first) 2. **Detail View**: Shows artifact with type-specific renderer **Detail Renderers**: - `PlanViewer` - Markdown in `
` with whitespace-pre-wrap
- `MapView` - Uses `MermaidRenderer`, optional SVG details
- `AnimationViewer` - Uses `AnimationPlayer` component
- `BenchmarkViewer` - Score display, report, expandable trace
- `EvolutionViewer` - Best score, report, expandable trace
- `VerificationViewer` - Checklist markdown

---

### **components/MermaidRenderer.tsx** (Diagram Rendering - 134 lines)
**React Component**: `MermaidRenderer({ content, className? })`

**Features**:
- Dynamic import of mermaid library
- Cache by first 100 chars of content
- Initializes mermaid with theme='dark', securityLevel='loose'
- Extracts code from markdown code blocks (```mermaid...```)
- Generates unique ID for each render
- Error boundary with fallback

**Secondary Components**:
- `MermaidMessageRenderer` - Extracts mermaid from chat message
- `useMermaidBlocks` - Hook to find all mermaid blocks in text

---

### **components/AnimationPlayer.tsx** (Storyboard Viewer - 179 lines)
**React Component**: `AnimationPlayer({ scene?, manifest?, storyboard?, className? })`

**Features**:
- Parses storyboard into scene sections (split by `## Scene`)
- Parses manifest JSON metadata
- Two-panel layout: Storyboard sidebar + preview area
- Scene selection buttons
- Toggle storyboard visibility

**Secondary Component**: `AnimationPreview` - Collapsible wrapper

**Limitations**: Stub for actual Manim-web execution (displays code/storyboard only)

---

### **components/model-selector.ts** (Model Picker Dialog - 400+ lines)
**Lit.js Web Component**: `KeatingModelSelector extends HTMLElement`

**Features**:
- Dialog overlay with model list
- Groups: Browser, Cloud, Custom
- WebGPU detection with capability check
- Model search/filter by name, ID, provider
- Loads from `getSelectableModels()` discovery
- Badges for capabilities (WebGPU, Cloud, Custom, Vision, Thinking)
- Status indicators (loading, browser model status, no WebGPU warning)

**Key Methods**:
- `static open(currentModel, onSelect)` - Factory method
- `checkWebGpu()` - Async adapter request
- `loadModels()` - Discovers all available models
- `getFilteredModels()` - Search filtering
- `renderGroup()`, `renderModelOption()` - Template methods

---

### **components/settings.ts** (Settings Dialog - partial)
**Lit.js Web Component**: `KeatingSettings extends HTMLElement`

**Features**:
- Provider list with API key inputs
- Shadow DOM encapsulation
- Loads keys from app storage on init
- Responsive modal dialog

---

### **components/custom-provider-dialog.ts** (Provider Config Dialog - partial)
**Purpose**: Form for adding/editing custom provider (Ollama, llama.cpp, Synthetic, etc.)

---

### **components/providers-models-tab.ts** (Lit-based Settings Tab - 90+ lines)
**Lit.js Component**: `KeatingProvidersModelsTab extends SettingsTab`

**Features**:
- Renders cloud provider key inputs
- Renders custom provider management
- Auto-discovery type handling (Ollama, llama.cpp, vLLM, LM Studio)
- OpenAI-compatible type handling
- Model listing and selection

---

## 3. BUILD CONFIGURATION

### **vite.config.ts**
```typescript
export default defineConfig({
  root: '.',
  publicDir: 'public',
  plugins: [
    react(),
    VitePWA({
      registerType: 'autoUpdate',
      manifest: { name: 'Keating - Hyperteacher', ... },
      workbox: {
        globPatterns: ['**/*.{js,css,html,ico,png,svg,woff2,woff}'],
        maximumFileSizeToCacheInBytes: 5 * 1024 * 1024,
        runtimeCaching: [
          { urlPattern: /\.(?:wasm|onnx)$/i, handler: 'CacheFirst', ... },
          { urlPattern: /^https:\/\/huggingface\.co\/.*/i, handler: 'CacheFirst', ... }
        ],
      },
    }),
  ],
  build: {
    outDir: 'dist',
    rollupOptions: {
      input: { main: 'index.html', chat: 'chat.html', tutorial: 'tutorial.html', blog: 'blog.html' }
    },
    assetsDir: 'assets',
    sourcemap: true,
  },
  server: {
    port: 3000,
    headers: {
      'Cross-Origin-Opener-Policy': 'same-origin',
      'Cross-Origin-Embedder-Policy': 'require-corp',
    },
  },
  base: './',
});
```

**Key Features**:
- Multi-entry build (4 HTML pages)
- PWA with auto-update service worker
- Special caching for WASM, ONNX, HuggingFace model files (30-day retention)
- WebGPU/SharedArrayBuffer headers for local dev
- Relative base path (`./`) for portability

### **tailwind.config.ts**
```typescript
const config: Config = {
  content: ['./index.html', './src/**/*.{ts,js}'],
  theme: {
    extend: {},
  },
  plugins: [],
};
```
- Minimal config, uses Tailwind defaults
- Scans HTML and source files

---

## 4. PACKAGE.json DEPENDENCIES

**Production**:
- `@huggingface/transformers` (^3.0.0) - Local model inference
- `@mariozechner/pi-agent-core` (^0.65.0) - Agent framework
- `@mariozechner/pi-ai` (^0.65.0) - LLM provider APIs
- `@mariozechner/pi-web-ui` (^0.65.0) - UI components (ChatPanel, SettingsDialog, etc.)
- `katex` (^0.16.44) - Math rendering
- `mermaid` (^11.4.0) - Diagram generation
- `react`, `react-dom` (^19.2.5)

**Dev**:
- `@vitejs/plugin-react` (^4.0.0)
- `tailwindcss` (^4.0.0)
- `typescript` (^5.7.0)
- `vite` (^5.4.0)
- `vite-plugin-pwa` (^1.2.0)
- `workbox-window` (^7.4.0)

---

## 5. STYLING & THEME

### **app.css**
- Imports base styles from `@mariozechner/pi-web-ui`
- Custom color override: `--color-primary: #6366f1` (indigo)
- Message editor fixes (text color, caret, placeholder opacity)
- Mobile-friendly enhancements:
  - Min 44px touch targets
  - 16px font size to prevent iOS zoom
  - Increased gaps (0.5rem → 1rem on mobile)
  - Full-width buttons in dialogs
  - Larger select/combobox elements (48px min-height)

### **Design System** (from index.html):
- **Color Variables**:
  - Paper: `#f4f1ea` (off-white, default background)
  - Ink: `#1a1a1a` (near-black, text)
  - Accent: `#d44a3d` (red-orange, highlights)
  - Terminal: `#0c0c0c` (darker black, code blocks)
  - Primary (Tailwind): `#6366f1` (indigo, interactive)
- **Typography**:
  - Body: `Space Mono` (monospace, 400/700 weights)
  - Terminal/UI: `VT323` (pixel font)
- **Retro Effects**:
  - Paper texture overlay (SVG noise, 4% opacity)
  - CRT scanlines (1px repeating, 3% opacity)
  - Paper fold shadow (inset radial gradient)
  - Distressed borders (clip-path polygon with jagged edges)
  - Status LED pulse (2s cubic-bezier animation, 0.3-1.0 opacity)

---

## 6. ARCHITECTURE SUMMARY

### **Execution Flow**:
1. **Browser loads** `index.html`
2. **React mounts** App.tsx at `#root`
3. **App.tsx renders** `#header` and `#chat-container` divs
4. **useEffect in App.tsx** dynamically imports `main.ts` and calls `initApp()`
5. **initApp()** checks WebGPU, initializes storage, creates agent, renders Lit.js components into divs
6. **Lit.js ChatPanel** manages chat loop, message display, and UI updates
7. **Agent dispatches** to hybrid stream function (routes browser model ↔ cloud provider)
8. **Browser model** streams from Transformers.js (ONNX Gemma 4 E4B)
9. **Cloud models** stream from Pi framework connectors (Google, Anthropic, OpenAI, etc.)

### **Key Architectural Decisions**:
- **Hybrid React + Lit.js**: React root layout, Lit.js for interactive components (ChatPanel, dialogs, model selector)
- **IndexedDB Backend**: All artifacts and learner state persisted client-side
- **PWA with Offline**: Service worker caches WASM, ONNX, HuggingFace model files for offline chat
- **Custom Provider Discovery**: Dynamically queries Ollama, llama.cpp, vLLM, LM Studio endpoints
- **WebGPU Capability Detection**: Graceful fallback to cloud models if WebGPU unavailable
- **Modular Tool System**: Agent tools are pure functions, saved artifacts enable composition

---

## 7. READY FOR REACT REWRITE

The codebase is **well-structured for full React conversion**:

**What to Port**:
- Main layout & state management (main.ts) → React hooks + context
- Agent lifecycle & ChatPanel integration → Custom React components
- Artifact browser → React component tree
- Settings dialogs → React modal system
- Model selector → React dropdown/modal

**No Hard Dependencies on Lit.js**:
- ChatPanel is a single WC, can be wrapped in React or replaced
- Storage, agent, tools are framework-agnostic
- HTML pages (tutorial, blog) are static (can become routes)

**Key Files to Refactor**:
1. `main.ts` → `useAgent()` hook + `AgentContext`
2. `components/*.ts` (Lit) → `components/*.tsx` (React)
3. App layout → Proper React component hierarchy
4. Vite config remains largely unchanged

This structure supports a phased migration with no breaking changes to the Pi framework integration.

Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding of the project. Let me write a thorough report: ## Comprehensive Keating Web Frontend Analysis ### PROJECT OVERVIEW Keating is a hyperteacher web application built on the Pi agent framework. It's a Progressive Web App (PWA) that supports both cloud LLM providers and local browser-based model inference via WebGPU. The app implements Socratic teaching methodology with diagnostic-first approach, supporting lesson planning, concept mapping, animation generation, verification checklists, and teaching policy evolution. --- ## 1. HTML FILES STRUCTURE ### **index.html** (`/home/diogenes/Projects/keating/web/index.html`) - **Purpose**: Main entry point for the React app - **Structure**: - CDN imports: Tailwind CSS (`cdn.tailwindcss.com`) - Google Fonts: `VT323` (terminal font) and `Space Mono` (monospace body) - Root element: `
` - Entry point: `/src/main-react.tsx` (module script) - **Style System**: - Extensive inline CSS with retro/terminal aesthetic: - CSS variables: `--paper` (#f4f1ea), `--ink` (#1a1a1a), `--accent` (#d44a3d), `--terminal` (#0c0c0c) - Paper texture overlay (SVG noise filter) - CRT scanline effect with `repeating-linear-gradient` - Blinking cursor animation - Typewriter effect - Glitch hover effect - Boot sequence animation - Paper fold shadow effect with `::before` pseudo-element - Distressed border with clip-path polygon - Status LED pulse animation - Button press retro effect - **Meta Tags**: PWA config (apple-mobile-web-app-capable, theme-color) ### **chat.html** (`/home/diogenes/Projects/keating/web/chat.html`) - **Purpose**: Chat interface page (appears to be deprecated/legacy) - **Structure**: - Same styling framework as index.html - Root element: `
` - Entry point: `/src/main-react.tsx` (module script) - **No interactive JS logic** - delegates to React ### **tutorial.html** (`/home/diogenes/Projects/keating/web/tutorial.html`) - **Purpose**: Model setup and API configuration guide - **Sections**: 1. **Navigation**: Fixed header with "KEATING//" logo and links to [HOME] [BLOG] 2. **Model Types Overview**: Two-column grid showing Browser vs Local options 3. **Detailed Tabs** (vanilla JS tab switching): - [BROWSER] - WebGPU zero-setup (Gemma 4 E4B, 5GB cache, offline support) - [OLLAMA] - Local LLM runner with GPU support (install, pull model, set endpoint) - [LLAMA.CPP] - Lightweight C++ inference (build, download GGUF, run server) - [LITELLM] - Unified API proxy (install pip, set env vars, run on port 4000) - [CLOUD] - Managed services (Google AI, Synthetic, Anthropic, OpenAI with API key setup) 4. **Security Note**: Explains local storage of API keys in IndexedDB or `~/.keating/.env` - **Interactive JS**: ```javascript document.querySelectorAll('.tab-btn').forEach(btn => { btn.addEventListener('click', () => { // Toggle active class on buttons and tab-content divs document.querySelectorAll('.tab-btn').forEach(b => b.classList.remove('active')); document.querySelectorAll('.tab-content').forEach(c => c.classList.remove('active')); btn.classList.add('active'); document.getElementById('tab-' + btn.dataset.tab).classList.add('active'); }); }); ``` - **External Dependencies**: Google Fonts, Tailwind CDN - **Terminal styling**: `.terminal-window` class with green-on-black aesthetic ### **blog.html** (`/home/diogenes/Projects/keating/web/blog.html`) - **Purpose**: Development log and release notes - **Structure**: 1. **Navigation**: Same fixed header 2. **Header Section**: "Keating Updates" title with description 3. **Article Cards** (10+ entries, most recent first): - Date badges with color-coded status (FIX, RELEASE, FEATURE, PWA, UPDATE, TECH, DEV LOG) - Post title, description, and markdown-formatted details - Code blocks with syntax highlighting (dark background) 4. **Footer**: Subscribe/GitHub link section - **Key Updates Logged**: - 2026-04-09: Chat model selector bug fix - 2026-04-09: v0.1.3 - Synthetic provider support + mobile UI polish - 2025-04-05: Chat panel fix & loading animation - 2025-04-05: PWA with browser model integration - 2025-04-05: WebGPU detection - 2025-01-15: Local model support via WebGPU - 2025-01-10: v0.1.0 - Initial release - **No interactive JS** - static HTML/CSS --- ## 2. REACT/TYPESCRIPT SOURCE FILES ### **Entry Point: main-react.tsx** ```typescript import React from "react"; import ReactDOM from "react-dom/client"; import { App } from "./App"; ReactDOM.createRoot(document.getElementById("root")!).render( ); ``` - Bootstraps React at DOM root - Renders `` component with strict mode ### **App.tsx** (React Root Component) ```typescript export function App() { const initializedRef = useRef(false); useEffect(() => { if (initializedRef.current) return; initializedRef.current = true; // Lazy-load main.ts initialization import("./main").then(({ initApp }) => { initApp(); }).catch(console.error); }, []); return (
); } ``` - **Purpose**: Wrapper that sets up container structure - **Behavior**: Lazy-loads `main.ts` on mount (dynamic import) - **Layout**: Flexbox column with header (fixed height) and chat (flex-1) - **Key Detail**: Uses Lit.js for rendering into these divs, not React components ### **main.ts** (Core Application Logic - 366 lines) **Imports**: - Pi Framework: `Agent`, `ChatPanel`, `SettingsDialog`, `ProviderKeysStore`, `CustomProvidersStore`, `SessionsStore`, etc. - Local: Custom components (`KeatingModelSelector`, `KeatingProvidersModelsTab`), storage, tools, browser-tools **Key State/Variables**: - `settings`, `providerKeys`, `sessions`, `customProviders` - Pi storage stores - `backend` - IndexedDB storage with 5 object stores - `currentSessionId`, `currentTitle`, `agent`, `chatPanel` - App state - `selectedModel` - Currently selected LLM - `webGpuAvailable` - Boolean for WebGPU capability **BROWSER_MODEL Constant**: ```typescript const BROWSER_MODEL: Model = { id: "gemma-4-e4b", name: "Gemma 4 E4B (Browser)", api: "browser" as Api, provider: "browser", baseUrl: "", reasoning: false, input: ["text"], cost: { input: 0, output: 0, cacheRead: 0, cacheWrite: 0 }, contextWindow: 0, maxTokens: 0, }; ``` **Core Functions**: 1. **createBrowserStreamFn()** - Hybrid streaming for local model - Extracts user messages from context - Builds prompt with system + conversation - Calls `localModel.generate()` with token streaming callback - Returns event stream with start/text_start/end events - Handles abort signals and errors 2. **hybridStreamFn()** - Dispatcher - Routes to browser model if provider === "browser" - Otherwise uses `streamSimple()` for cloud providers 3. **renderApp()** - Lit.js rendering - Renders header with title, theme toggle, settings button - Appends ChatPanel to chat-container (once only, no re-render) - Uses Lit's `html` and `render` functions 4. **switchModel()** - Model selection handler - Loads browser model if needed - Updates selectedModel - Recreates agent with new model 5. **createAgent()** - Agent factory - Loads Keating tools via `createKeatingTools(keatingStorage)` - Sets initial state (systemPrompt, model, thinkingLevel, messages, tools) - Configures stream function to hybrid dispatcher - Subscribes to agent state updates - Calls `chatPanel.setAgent()` with callbacks for API key prompts, model selection 6. **initApp()** - Startup sequence - Checks WebGPU capability - Syncs custom provider keys - Initializes KeatingStorage - Creates ChatPanel - Creates initial agent - Renders app 7. **checkBrowserModelCapability()** - WebGPU detection - Checks `navigator.gpu` existence - Attempts to request adapter - Logs results to console --- ### **keating/storage.ts** (IndexedDB-based Artifact Storage) **Database**: `keating-db` (v1) **Object Stores**: - `lesson-plans` - Structured lesson content by topic - `lesson-maps` - Mermaid diagram content - `animations` - Storyboard, scene code, manifest (Manim-web compatible) - `verifications` - Fact-checking checklists - `benchmarks` - Synthetic learner benchmark results with traces - `evolutions` - Policy evolution runs with best scores - `policies` - Teaching policy documents (one active) - `feedback` - User feedback (thumbs-up/down/confused) entries - `learner-state` - Learner progress profile (topics, feedback history, strengths/weaknesses) **Key Methods**: - Generic: `init()`, `getStore()`, `getAll()`, `getByTopic()`, `put()`, `generateId()` - Domain-specific: `saveLessonPlan()`, `getLessonPlans()`, `saveLessonMap()`, etc. - Learner: `recordFeedback()`, `getLearnerState()`, `saveLearnerState()` - Batch: `listArtifacts()` - aggregates all artifacts sorted by createdAt **Interfaces** (TypeScript): ```typescript interface LessonPlan { id, topic, createdAt, updatedAt, content, metadata? } interface LessonMap { id, topic, createdAt, mmdContent, svgContent? } interface Animation { id, topic, createdAt, storyboard, scene, manifest } interface BenchmarkResult { id, topic?, createdAt, score, trace?, report } interface EvolutionResult { id, topic?, createdAt, bestScore, policy, trace?, report } interface LearnerState { topicsExplored[], feedbackHistory[], strengths[], weaknesses[], lastSessionAt?, sessionsCount } ``` **Exports**: `DEFAULT_BROWSER_POLICY` - Markdown policy template --- ### **keating/browser-tools.ts** (Agent Tools - 534 lines) **System Prompt**: `KEATING_SYSTEM_PROMPT` - 57-line prompt with teaching philosophy and available commands **Tools** (13 total, each a `AgentTool` with JSON schema): 1. **`/plan `** - Generate structured lesson plan - Calls `buildLessonPlan()` from core.ts - Saves to IndexedDB via `KeatingStorage.saveLessonPlan()` - Returns markdown formatted plan 2. **`/map `** - Create Mermaid concept map - Calls `buildConceptMap()` from core.ts - Returns markdown code block with mermaid syntax 3. **`/animate `** - Generate animation storyboard - Calls `resolveTopic()` to get topic definition - Generates 6-scene storyboard (Intro, Intuition, Formal, Misconceptions, Examples, Transfer) - Creates Manim-web compatible scene code - Generates JSON manifest with metadata 4. **`/verify `** - Create verification checklist - Generates markdown checklist with Core Facts, Misconceptions, Prerequisites, Edge Cases, Sources sections - Uses prerequisite/misconception data from topic definitions 5. **`/bench [topic]`** - Run synthetic learner benchmark - Calls `runBenchmarkSuite()` from core.ts - Saves to storage with score and trace - Reports overall score and detailed breakdown 6. **`/evolve [topic]`** - Evolve teaching policy - Calls `evolvePolicy()` from core.ts - Saves new policy as active if it improves over baseline - Reports best score, candidates accepted ratio 7. **`/feedback [topic]`** - Record learning signal - Maps string signals to enum values - Calls `recordFeedback()` which updates learner state 8. **`/policy`** - Show current teaching policy - Retrieves active policy from storage - Falls back to `DEFAULT_BROWSER_POLICY` 9. **`/outputs`** - Browse saved artifacts - Calls `listArtifacts()` aggregator - Returns list of last 20 artifacts with dates 10. **`/state`** - Show learner profile - Calls `getLearnerState()` - Reports topics explored, feedback history (counts), last session 11. **`/improve`** - Get improvement suggestions - Calls `diagnoseBenchmark()` from core.ts - Returns targeted suggestions for weak areas 12. **`/trace`** - Browse benchmark/evolution history - Retrieves all benchmarks and evolutions - Filters by type (all/benchmark/evolution) - Lists last 10 of each 13. **`/prompt-eval `** - Evaluate teaching prompt - Calls `evaluatePrompt()` from core.ts - Returns score and objective vector breakdown **Helper**: `createSimpleTool()` - Wraps functions with AgentTool schema --- ### **keating/core.ts** (Teaching Engine - 400+ lines, partially shown) **Types**: - `Domain` - enum: math, science, philosophy, code, law, politics, psychology, medicine, arts, history, general - `TopicDefinition` - Complete topic with summary, intuition, formal core, prerequisites, misconceptions, examples, exercises, reflections, diagram nodes - `TeacherPolicy` - 10 float/int parameters (analogyDensity, socraticRatio, formalism, retrievalPractice, exerciseCount, diagramBias, reflectionBias, interdisciplinaryBias, challengeRate) - `LessonPhase` - id, title, purpose, bullets[] - `LessonPlan` - topic, policy, phases[] - `LearnerProfile` - 8 traits (priorKnowledge, abstractionComfort, etc.) - `TeachingSimulation` - Result of running lesson against synthetic learner - `TopicBenchmark` - Aggregated results across learner profiles - `BenchmarkResult` - Full suite results with overallScore - `EvolutionCandidate` - Policy variant with acceptance decision and parametric changes - `PromptObjectiveVector` - 6 teaching objectives scored 0-1 **Functions** (partially listed): - `buildLessonPlan(topic, policy)` - Generate lesson phases - `lessonPlanToMarkdown(plan)` - Format as markdown - `buildConceptMap(topic)` - Generate Mermaid diagram - `runBenchmarkSuite(policy, topic?)` - Run teaching simulations - `benchmarkToMarkdown(result)` - Format benchmark report - `evolvePolicy(basePolicy, topic?)` - Genetic/evolutionary search - `evolutionToMarkdown(run)` - Format evolution report - `diagnoseBenchmark(result)` - Extract improvement suggestions - `evaluatePrompt(promptContent)` - Score against objectives - `resolveTopic(topicName)` - Lookup topic definition **Constant**: `DEFAULT_POLICY` - Default teacher policy parameters **Topics Database**: Large `TOPICS` object with 10+ hardcoded topics (derivative, entropy, bayes-rule, etc.) with full definitions --- ### **stores/local-model.ts** (WebGPU Model Management - 156 lines) **Exports**: `localModel` singleton store, `LocalModel` interface **LocalModelStore Class**: **State**: ```typescript interface LocalModel { loaded: boolean; loading: boolean; loadingProgress: number; error: string | null; model: any | null; processor: any | null; } ``` **Methods**: 1. **`subscribe(listener)`** - Observer pattern, returns unsubscribe function 2. **`load()`** - Async model loading - Imports `AutoProcessor`, `AutoModelForCausalLM` from `@huggingface/transformers` - Model ID: `onnx-community/gemma-4-E4B-it-ONNX` (ONNX format for Transformers.js) - Loads with dtype='q4f16', device='webgpu' - Emits progress callbacks (progress_callback) - Updates state and notifies listeners 3. **`generate(prompt, options?, onToken?)`** - Text generation - Formats prompt with chat template - Tokenizes input - Uses `TextStreamer` for token-by-token callback - Generates with max_new_tokens, temperature, do_sample=true - Returns decoded output as single string **Config**: - `allowLocalModels = false` - `useBrowserCache = true` --- ### **lib/provider-models.ts** (Custom Provider Discovery - 338 lines) **Types**: ```typescript type KeatingCustomProviderType = "ollama" | "llama.cpp" | "vllm" | "lmstudio" | "openai-completions" | "openai-responses" | "anthropic-messages" | "synthetic" type KeatingCustomProvider = Omit & { type: KeatingCustomProviderType } ``` **Discovery Functions**: 1. **`discoverOpenAiCompatibleModels(provider)`** - Tries both `{baseUrl}` and `{baseUrl}/v1` - Fetches `/models` endpoint - Parses OpenAI API schema - Infers context window, max tokens, vision, reasoning 2. **`discoverOllamaModels(baseUrl)`** - Uses `ollama/browser` client - Calls `ollama.list()` and `ollama.show()` for each model - Filters by `capabilities.includes("tools")` - Extracts architecture-specific context length 3. **`discoverLlamaCppModels(baseUrl, apiKey?)`** - Fetches `/v1/models` - Parses llama.cpp schema (context_length, max_tokens) 4. **`discoverVllmModels(baseUrl, apiKey?)`** - Fetches `/v1/models` - Uses `max_model_len` for context 5. **`discoverLmStudioModels(baseUrl)`** - Fetches `/v1/models` - Supports context_length, max_context_length, max_model_len variants 6. **`discoverCustomProviderModels(provider)`** - Routes to appropriate discovery function - Falls back to manual `provider.models` array if provided **Public Exports**: - `getCustomProviders()` - From app storage - `syncCustomProviderKeys()` - Cache API keys from providers - `getProviderApiKey(providerName)` - Lookup stored key - `getSelectableModels()` - Aggregates all models (cloud + custom) --- ### **components/ProvidersModelsTab.tsx** (Settings Tab - 65 lines) **Purpose**: Provider configuration UI in settings dialog (Lit.js web component) **Class**: `KeatingProvidersModelsTab extends SettingsTab` **Methods**: - `connectedCallback()` - Load custom providers on mount - `getTabName()` - Returns "Providers & Models" - `render()` - Returns JSX with provider list **Limitations**: Stub implementation (loadCustomProviders is empty) --- ### **components/ArtifactViewer.tsx** (Artifact Browser - 233 lines) **React Component**: `ArtifactViewer({ storage, artifactId?, onClose? })` **State**: - `artifacts` - Aggregated list of all artifact types - `selected` - Currently selected artifact for detail view - `loading` - Fetch state **Artifact Types**: plan, map, animation, benchmark, evolution, verification **UI Modes**: 1. **List View**: Clickable cards for each artifact, sorted by date (newest first) 2. **Detail View**: Shows artifact with type-specific renderer **Detail Renderers**: - `PlanViewer` - Markdown in `
` with whitespace-pre-wrap
- `MapView` - Uses `MermaidRenderer`, optional SVG details
- `AnimationViewer` - Uses `AnimationPlayer` component
- `BenchmarkViewer` - Score display, report, expandable trace
- `EvolutionViewer` - Best score, report, expandable trace
- `VerificationViewer` - Checklist markdown

---

### **components/MermaidRenderer.tsx** (Diagram Rendering - 134 lines)
**React Component**: `MermaidRenderer({ content, className? })`

**Features**:
- Dynamic import of mermaid library
- Cache by first 100 chars of content
- Initializes mermaid with theme='dark', securityLevel='loose'
- Extracts code from markdown code blocks (```mermaid...```)
- Generates unique ID for each render
- Error boundary with fallback

**Secondary Components**:
- `MermaidMessageRenderer` - Extracts mermaid from chat message
- `useMermaidBlocks` - Hook to find all mermaid blocks in text

---

### **components/AnimationPlayer.tsx** (Storyboard Viewer - 179 lines)
**React Component**: `AnimationPlayer({ scene?, manifest?, storyboard?, className? })`

**Features**:
- Parses storyboard into scene sections (split by `## Scene`)
- Parses manifest JSON metadata
- Two-panel layout: Storyboard sidebar + preview area
- Scene selection buttons
- Toggle storyboard visibility

**Secondary Component**: `AnimationPreview` - Collapsible wrapper

**Limitations**: Stub for actual Manim-web execution (displays code/storyboard only)

---

### **components/model-selector.ts** (Model Picker Dialog - 400+ lines)
**Lit.js Web Component**: `KeatingModelSelector extends HTMLElement`

**Features**:
- Dialog overlay with model list
- Groups: Browser, Cloud, Custom
- WebGPU detection with capability check
- Model search/filter by name, ID, provider
- Loads from `getSelectableModels()` discovery
- Badges for capabilities (WebGPU, Cloud, Custom, Vision, Thinking)
- Status indicators (loading, browser model status, no WebGPU warning)

**Key Methods**:
- `static open(currentModel, onSelect)` - Factory method
- `checkWebGpu()` - Async adapter request
- `loadModels()` - Discovers all available models
- `getFilteredModels()` - Search filtering
- `renderGroup()`, `renderModelOption()` - Template methods

---

### **components/settings.ts** (Settings Dialog - partial)
**Lit.js Web Component**: `KeatingSettings extends HTMLElement`

**Features**:
- Provider list with API key inputs
- Shadow DOM encapsulation
- Loads keys from app storage on init
- Responsive modal dialog

---

### **components/custom-provider-dialog.ts** (Provider Config Dialog - partial)
**Purpose**: Form for adding/editing custom provider (Ollama, llama.cpp, Synthetic, etc.)

---

### **components/providers-models-tab.ts** (Lit-based Settings Tab - 90+ lines)
**Lit.js Component**: `KeatingProvidersModelsTab extends SettingsTab`

**Features**:
- Renders cloud provider key inputs
- Renders custom provider management
- Auto-discovery type handling (Ollama, llama.cpp, vLLM, LM Studio)
- OpenAI-compatible type handling
- Model listing and selection

---

## 3. BUILD CONFIGURATION

### **vite.config.ts**
```typescript
export default defineConfig({
  root: '.',
  publicDir: 'public',
  plugins: [
    react(),
    VitePWA({
      registerType: 'autoUpdate',
      manifest: { name: 'Keating - Hyperteacher', ... },
      workbox: {
        globPatterns: ['**/*.{js,css,html,ico,png,svg,woff2,woff}'],
        maximumFileSizeToCacheInBytes: 5 * 1024 * 1024,
        runtimeCaching: [
          { urlPattern: /\.(?:wasm|onnx)$/i, handler: 'CacheFirst', ... },
          { urlPattern: /^https:\/\/huggingface\.co\/.*/i, handler: 'CacheFirst', ... }
        ],
      },
    }),
  ],
  build: {
    outDir: 'dist',
    rollupOptions: {
      input: { main: 'index.html', chat: 'chat.html', tutorial: 'tutorial.html', blog: 'blog.html' }
    },
    assetsDir: 'assets',
    sourcemap: true,
  },
  server: {
    port: 3000,
    headers: {
      'Cross-Origin-Opener-Policy': 'same-origin',
      'Cross-Origin-Embedder-Policy': 'require-corp',
    },
  },
  base: './',
});
```

**Key Features**:
- Multi-entry build (4 HTML pages)
- PWA with auto-update service worker
- Special caching for WASM, ONNX, HuggingFace model files (30-day retention)
- WebGPU/SharedArrayBuffer headers for local dev
- Relative base path (`./`) for portability

### **tailwind.config.ts**
```typescript
const config: Config = {
  content: ['./index.html', './src/**/*.{ts,js}'],
  theme: {
    extend: {},
  },
  plugins: [],
};
```
- Minimal config, uses Tailwind defaults
- Scans HTML and source files

---

## 4. PACKAGE.json DEPENDENCIES

**Production**:
- `@huggingface/transformers` (^3.0.0) - Local model inference
- `@mariozechner/pi-agent-core` (^0.65.0) - Agent framework
- `@mariozechner/pi-ai` (^0.65.0) - LLM provider APIs
- `@mariozechner/pi-web-ui` (^0.65.0) - UI components (ChatPanel, SettingsDialog, etc.)
- `katex` (^0.16.44) - Math rendering
- `mermaid` (^11.4.0) - Diagram generation
- `react`, `react-dom` (^19.2.5)

**Dev**:
- `@vitejs/plugin-react` (^4.0.0)
- `tailwindcss` (^4.0.0)
- `typescript` (^5.7.0)
- `vite` (^5.4.0)
- `vite-plugin-pwa` (^1.2.0)
- `workbox-window` (^7.4.0)

---

## 5. STYLING & THEME

### **app.css**
- Imports base styles from `@mariozechner/pi-web-ui`
- Custom color override: `--color-primary: #6366f1` (indigo)
- Message editor fixes (text color, caret, placeholder opacity)
- Mobile-friendly enhancements:
  - Min 44px touch targets
  - 16px font size to prevent iOS zoom
  - Increased gaps (0.5rem → 1rem on mobile)
  - Full-width buttons in dialogs
  - Larger select/combobox elements (48px min-height)

### **Design System** (from index.html):
- **Color Variables**:
  - Paper: `#f4f1ea` (off-white, default background)
  - Ink: `#1a1a1a` (near-black, text)
  - Accent: `#d44a3d` (red-orange, highlights)
  - Terminal: `#0c0c0c` (darker black, code blocks)
  - Primary (Tailwind): `#6366f1` (indigo, interactive)
- **Typography**:
  - Body: `Space Mono` (monospace, 400/700 weights)
  - Terminal/UI: `VT323` (pixel font)
- **Retro Effects**:
  - Paper texture overlay (SVG noise, 4% opacity)
  - CRT scanlines (1px repeating, 3% opacity)
  - Paper fold shadow (inset radial gradient)
  - Distressed borders (clip-path polygon with jagged edges)
  - Status LED pulse (2s cubic-bezier animation, 0.3-1.0 opacity)

---

## 6. ARCHITECTURE SUMMARY

### **Execution Flow**:
1. **Browser loads** `index.html`
2. **React mounts** App.tsx at `#root`
3. **App.tsx renders** `#header` and `#chat-container` divs
4. **useEffect in App.tsx** dynamically imports `main.ts` and calls `initApp()`
5. **initApp()** checks WebGPU, initializes storage, creates agent, renders Lit.js components into divs
6. **Lit.js ChatPanel** manages chat loop, message display, and UI updates
7. **Agent dispatches** to hybrid stream function (routes browser model ↔ cloud provider)
8. **Browser model** streams from Transformers.js (ONNX Gemma 4 E4B)
9. **Cloud models** stream from Pi framework connectors (Google, Anthropic, OpenAI, etc.)

### **Key Architectural Decisions**:
- **Hybrid React + Lit.js**: React root layout, Lit.js for interactive components (ChatPanel, dialogs, model selector)
- **IndexedDB Backend**: All artifacts and learner state persisted client-side
- **PWA with Offline**: Service worker caches WASM, ONNX, HuggingFace model files for offline chat
- **Custom Provider Discovery**: Dynamically queries Ollama, llama.cpp, vLLM, LM Studio endpoints
- **WebGPU Capability Detection**: Graceful fallback to cloud models if WebGPU unavailable
- **Modular Tool System**: Agent tools are pure functions, saved artifacts enable composition

---

## 7. READY FOR REACT REWRITE

The codebase is **well-structured for full React conversion**:

**What to Port**:
- Main layout & state management (main.ts) → React hooks + context
- Agent lifecycle & ChatPanel integration → Custom React components
- Artifact browser → React component tree
- Settings dialogs → React modal system
- Model selector → React dropdown/modal

**No Hard Dependencies on Lit.js**:
- ChatPanel is a single WC, can be wrapped in React or replaced
- Storage, agent, tools are framework-agnostic
- HTML pages (tutorial, blog) are static (can become routes)

**Key Files to Refactor**:
1. `main.ts` → `useAgent()` hook + `AgentContext`
2. `components/*.ts` (Lit) → `components/*.tsx` (React)
3. App layout → Proper React component hierarchy
4. Vite config remains largely unchanged

This structure supports a phased migration with no breaking changes to the Pi framework integration.

Good, I have a full picture now. Here's the plan:

**Current state:**
- `index.html` / `chat.html` → React chat app (App.tsx → Lit.js ChatPanel)  
- `tutorial.html`, `blog.html` → standalone vanilla HTML pages
- `main.ts` → Lit.js mixed with logic (needs splitting)

**Rewrite plan:**
1. Add `react-router-dom` → SPA with routes `/`, `/chat`, `/tutorial`, `/blog`
2. Extract the retro CSS into a proper stylesheet (no more CDN Tailwind)
3. Convert landing page → `src/pages/Landing.tsx`
4. Convert tutorial → `src/pages/Tutorial.tsx`
5. Convert blog → `src/pages/Blog.tsx`
6. Wrap chat logic → `src/pages/Chat.tsx`
7. Shared `Nav` + `Footer` components
8. Update `App.tsx` as the router root, single `index.html`
9. Update vite.config to single entry

Let me read the remaining files I need before implementing:

KEATING//
[HOME] [BLOG]

Model Setup Guide

Choose how Keating runs AI models

BROWSER ZERO SETUP

Runs entirely in your browser using WebGPU. No installation, no API keys, no server. Just open and chat.

  • - Uses Transformers.js + ONNX models
  • - Model cached in browser (~5GB)
  • - Works offline after first load
  • - Privacy: data never leaves device

LOCAL REQUIRES SETUP

Run any model locally with Ollama, llama.cpp, LiteLLM, or llmfit. More model choices, better performance.

  • - Use any GGUF model
  • - GPU acceleration (CUDA/Metal)
  • - No internet required
  • - Set endpoint in settings

Browser WebGPU (Zero Setup)

The simplest option - just use Keating in a supported browser. No installation required.

# Requirements:

Chrome 113+ / Edge 113+ / Firefox Nightly (WebGPU flag)

GPU with WebGPU support (most modern GPUs)

~5GB free disk space for model cache

01. Open Keating web app in Chrome or Edge
02. Select "Gemma 4 E4B (Browser)" as model
03. Wait for model to download and cache (~5GB, one-time)
04. Chat! Works offline for future sessions

NO_API_KEY_REQUIRED

Your conversations never leave your device. Completely private.

Ollama

Popular local LLM runner with excellent GPU support. Works with any GGUF model.

# Install Ollama:

curl -fsSL https://ollama.com/install.sh | sh

# Pull a model:

ollama pull gemma3:4b

# Or use a custom GGUF:

ollama create mymodel -f Modelfile

# Start server (runs on port 11434):

ollama serve

01. Install Ollama from ollama.com
02. Pull your preferred model: ollama pull gemma3:4b
03. In Keating settings, add custom provider:

Provider: ollama

Base URL: http://localhost:11434

Model: gemma3:4b (or your model name)

GPU_ACCELERATION

Ollama auto-detects CUDA (NVIDIA) and Metal (macOS). No API key needed for local.

llama.cpp

Lightweight C++ inference. Maximum control and performance. Runs any GGUF model.

# Clone and build:

git clone https://github.com/ggerganov/llama.cpp

cd llama.cpp && make

# Download a GGUF model:

wget https://huggingface.co/unsloth/gemma-4-E4B-it-GGUF/resolve/main/gemma-4-E4B-it-UD-Q4_K_XL.gguf

# Run server:

./llama-server -m gemma-4-E4B-it-UD-Q4_K_XL.gguf --port 8080

01. Build llama.cpp from GitHub
02. Download a GGUF model from HuggingFace
03. Start the server with your model
04. In Keating settings, add custom provider pointing to http://localhost:8080

TIP

Use -ngl 99 to offload all layers to GPU. Use -c 8192 for larger context.

LiteLLM

Unified API proxy that works with 100+ LLM providers. Exposes an OpenAI-compatible endpoint.

# Install:

pip install litellm

# Run with a local model:

litellm --model ollama/gemma3:4b

# Or with API keys (env vars):

export OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-...

export ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-ant-...

litellm --port 4000

01. Install: pip install litellm
02. Set API keys as environment variables (if using cloud providers)
03. Start proxy: litellm --port 4000
04. Point Keating to http://localhost:4000

UNIFIED_API

LiteLLM gives you one OpenAI-compatible endpoint that can route to any provider (local or cloud).

Cloud Providers

Use managed AI services for best performance and model variety. Requires API keys.

Google AI Studio (Gemini)

  1. 1. Go to aistudio.google.com/app/apikey
  2. 2. Sign in and click "Create API Key"
  3. 3. Copy key (starts with "AIza...")
  4. 4. Paste in Keating settings

Free tier: 15 req/min, 1M tokens/day

Synthetic

  1. 1. Create or copy your Synthetic API key
  2. 2. In Keating settings, add a custom provider named synthetic
  3. 3. Set the provider type to Synthetic (OpenAI Compatible)
  4. 4. Use https://api.synthetic.new/openai/v1 as the base URL

Use this when you want Synthetic’s hosted models through an OpenAI-compatible endpoint.

Anthropic (Claude)

  1. 1. Go to console.anthropic.com
  2. 2. Create account and navigate to API Keys
  3. 3. Click "Create Key" and copy
  4. 4. Paste in Keating settings

Pricing: Claude Sonnet $3/M input, $15/M output

OpenAI (GPT)

  1. 1. Go to platform.openai.com/api-keys
  2. 2. Create account and click "Create new secret key"
  3. 3. Copy immediately (shown only once)
  4. 4. Paste in Keating settings

Pricing: GPT-4o $2.50/M input, $10/M output

SECURITY_NOTE

API keys are stored locally in your browser's IndexedDB (web) or ~/.keating/.env (CLI). They never leave your device. Never commit keys to git.

[BACK_TO_HOME]

Development log and release notes

86 2026-04-09 87 FIX 88
89

Chat Model Selector Now Reflects the Actual Choice

90

Fixed the web UI bug where the chat window kept showing gemini-3.1-pro-preview even after picking a different model. The selected model now updates the agent state directly, so the chat button and the runtime stay in sync.

91 92

What Changed

93
    94
  • State Sync: Selecting a model now writes the chosen model back into the active agent state instead of leaving the old Gemini placeholder in place.
    • 95
  • Immediate Rerender: The model button in the chat window refreshes as soon as a new model is chosen.
    • 96
  • Browser Path: The local browser model is now represented as a real model object, so the UI can display it explicitly instead of pretending everything is Gemini.
    • 97
98 99

This fixes the mismatch between the picker and the chat header, which made it look like model changes were being ignored.

100
101 102 103
104
105 2026-04-09 106 RELEASE 107
108

v0.1.3 - Synthetic Provider Support and Mobile UI Polish

109

Keating now exposes Synthetic as a first-class custom provider in the Pi settings flow. The provider is configured as an OpenAI-compatible endpoint at https://api.synthetic.new/openai/v1, with matching setup guidance in the tutorial.

110 111

Key Changes

112
    113
  • Synthetic Provider: Added a dedicated custom-provider entry so users can select Synthetic directly from the provider picker.
    • 114
  • Setup Guide: Updated web/tutorial.html with the exact provider name, type, and base URL.
    • 115
  • Mobile Polish: Increased touch targets and spacing across the homepage, model selector, settings dialog, and install tabs.
    • 116
117 118

This release keeps the UI consistent with the current provider flow while making the browser experience less cramped on small screens.

119
120 121 122
123
124 2025-04-05 125 FIX 126
127

Chat Panel Fix & Model Loading Animation

128

Fixed the text disappearing issue in the chat panel that occurred after sending messages. Also added a one-time model loading animation when the browser model initializes.

129 130

Bug Fix: Chat Panel Lifecycle

131

The root cause was Lit re-rendering replacing the ChatPanel element on every state update. Separated the static header from the dynamic chat panel with distinct DOM containers. The header renders via Lit's templating, while the ChatPanel is appended once and never replaced.

132 133

New: Model Loading Overlay

134

When using the browser model (Gemma 4 E4B), a loading overlay now appears on the homepage showing real-time download progress. This only shows once—the first time the model is loaded. Subsequent visits skip the overlay since the model is cached locally.

135 136
137 
// Model loading state communicated via postMessage
138	window.parent.postMessage({ type: 'model-loading-progress', progress: 45 }, '*');
139	
140	// Parent page shows overlay only once
141	const modelLoadShown = localStorage.getItem('keating_model_load_shown') === 'true';
142
143 144

The overlay includes a progress bar, percentage display, and explains that the ~2GB download only happens once.

145
146 147 148
149
150 2025-04-05 151 FEATURE 152 PWA 153
154

Installable PWA with Full Browser Model Integration

155

Keating is now a fully installable Progressive Web App. Install it from your browser and run it offline with the browser model. The WebGPU-powered Gemma 4 E4B model streams tokens in real-time directly in your browser.

156 157

Key Changes

158
    159
  • Browser Model → Agent: The local Gemma model is now fully wired into the Agent infrastructure. Token streaming works via a custom stream function that dispatches between WebGPU and cloud providers.
    • 160
  • Automatic Fallback: On load, the app checks for WebGPU support. If unavailable, it automatically falls back to gemini-3.1-pro-preview.
    • 161
  • Model Selector: The model selector now shows WebGPU status in real-time. Browser option is disabled with a clear message when WebGPU isn't available.
    • 162
  • PWA Manifest: Added service worker with intelligent caching for WASM files and model weights from HuggingFace CDN.
    • 163
164 165

How It Works

166
167 
// Hybrid stream function dispatches based on model selection
168	const hybridStreamFn = async (model, context, options) => {
169	  if (selectedModelId === 'browser' && webGpuAvailable) {
170	    return createBrowserStreamFn()(model, context, options);
171	  }
172	  return streamSimple(model, context, options);
173	};
174
175 176

Install: Visit keating.help in Chrome/Edge and click "Install" in the address bar, or use the browser's menu → "Install app".

177
178 179 180
181
182 2025-04-05 183 UPDATE 184
185

Default Cloud Model: Gemini 3.1 Pro Preview

186

Updated the default cloud model from gemini-2.5-pro to gemini-3.1-pro-preview. This gives access to the latest Gemini capabilities when WebGPU is unavailable.

187

The model selector UI has been updated to reflect this change, showing "Gemini 3.1 Pro Preview" instead of the previous version.

188
189 190 191
192
193 2025-04-05 194 TECH 195
196

Robust WebGPU Detection

197

The model selector now performs async WebGPU detection before rendering. This prevents the UI from showing the browser option when it won't actually work.

198 199
200 
async checkWebGpu(): Promise<boolean> {
201	  if (!navigator.gpu) return false;
202	  try {
203	    const adapter = await navigator.gpu.requestAdapter();
204	    return adapter !== null;
205	  } catch {
206	    return false;
207	  }
208	}
209
210 211

If WebGPU is unavailable, the browser model option shows "WebGPU not available" and is grayed out.

212
213 214 215
216
217 2025-01-15 218 FEATURE 219
220

Local Model Support via WebGPU

221

Keating now runs entirely in your browser using WebGPU. Run Gemma 4 E4B locally without any API keys. The model loads progressively and caches in your browser for subsequent sessions.

222

Requires Chrome 113+ or Edge 113+ with WebGPU support. Model size: ~5GB cached locally.

223

227

231

v0.1.0 - Initial Public Release

232

First public release of Keating hyperteacher. Built on the Pi agent framework with support for Google Gemini, Anthropic Claude, and OpenAI GPT models. Features Socratic teaching methodology with diagnostic-first approach.

233
    234
  • - Chat-based teaching interface

The Hyperteacher Philosophy

248

Keating is named after John Keating from Dead Poets Society, who taught that the purpose of education is not to fill minds but to ignite them. Our AI doesn't give answers - it forces you to reconstruct understanding from memory.

249

Core principle: struggle is the feature, not the bug. Neural pathways form through effort.

KEATING//
[HOME] [TUTORIAL]

Keating Updates

Development log and release notes

2026-04-09 FIX

Chat Model Selector Now Reflects the Actual Choice

Fixed the web UI bug where the chat window kept showing gemini-3.1-pro-preview even after picking a different model. The selected model now updates the agent state directly, so the chat button and the runtime stay in sync.

What Changed

  • State Sync: Selecting a model now writes the chosen model back into the active agent state instead of leaving the old Gemini placeholder in place.
  • Immediate Rerender: The model button in the chat window refreshes as soon as a new model is chosen.
  • Browser Path: The local browser model is now represented as a real model object, so the UI can display it explicitly instead of pretending everything is Gemini.

This fixes the mismatch between the picker and the chat header, which made it look like model changes were being ignored.

2026-04-09 RELEASE

v0.1.3 - Synthetic Provider Support and Mobile UI Polish

Keating now exposes Synthetic as a first-class custom provider in the Pi settings flow. The provider is configured as an OpenAI-compatible endpoint at https://api.synthetic.new/openai/v1, with matching setup guidance in the tutorial.

Key Changes

  • Synthetic Provider: Added a dedicated custom-provider entry so users can select Synthetic directly from the provider picker.
  • Setup Guide: Updated web/tutorial.html with the exact provider name, type, and base URL.
  • Mobile Polish: Increased touch targets and spacing across the homepage, model selector, settings dialog, and install tabs.

This release keeps the UI consistent with the current provider flow while making the browser experience less cramped on small screens.

2025-04-05 FIX

Chat Panel Fix & Model Loading Animation

Fixed the text disappearing issue in the chat panel that occurred after sending messages. Also added a one-time model loading animation when the browser model initializes.

Bug Fix: Chat Panel Lifecycle

The root cause was Lit re-rendering replacing the ChatPanel element on every state update. Separated the static header from the dynamic chat panel with distinct DOM containers. The header renders via Lit's templating, while the ChatPanel is appended once and never replaced.

New: Model Loading Overlay

When using the browser model (Gemma 4 E4B), a loading overlay now appears on the homepage showing real-time download progress. This only shows once—the first time the model is loaded. Subsequent visits skip the overlay since the model is cached locally.

// Model loading state communicated via postMessage
window.parent.postMessage({ type: 'model-loading-progress', progress: 45 }, '*');

// Parent page shows overlay only once
const modelLoadShown = localStorage.getItem('keating_model_load_shown') === 'true';

The overlay includes a progress bar, percentage display, and explains that the ~2GB download only happens once.

2025-04-05 FEATURE PWA

Installable PWA with Full Browser Model Integration

Keating is now a fully installable Progressive Web App. Install it from your browser and run it offline with the browser model. The WebGPU-powered Gemma 4 E4B model streams tokens in real-time directly in your browser.

Key Changes

  • Browser Model → Agent: The local Gemma model is now fully wired into the Agent infrastructure. Token streaming works via a custom stream function that dispatches between WebGPU and cloud providers.
  • Automatic Fallback: On load, the app checks for WebGPU support. If unavailable, it automatically falls back to gemini-3.1-pro-preview.
  • Model Selector: The model selector now shows WebGPU status in real-time. Browser option is disabled with a clear message when WebGPU isn't available.
  • PWA Manifest: Added service worker with intelligent caching for WASM files and model weights from HuggingFace CDN.

How It Works

// Hybrid stream function dispatches based on model selection
const hybridStreamFn = async (model, context, options) => {
  if (selectedModelId === 'browser' && webGpuAvailable) {
    return createBrowserStreamFn()(model, context, options);
  }
  return streamSimple(model, context, options);
};

Install: Visit keating.help in Chrome/Edge and click "Install" in the address bar, or use the browser's menu → "Install app".

2025-04-05 UPDATE

Default Cloud Model: Gemini 3.1 Pro Preview

Updated the default cloud model from gemini-2.5-pro to gemini-3.1-pro-preview. This gives access to the latest Gemini capabilities when WebGPU is unavailable.

The model selector UI has been updated to reflect this change, showing "Gemini 3.1 Pro Preview" instead of the previous version.

2025-04-05 TECH

Robust WebGPU Detection

The model selector now performs async WebGPU detection before rendering. This prevents the UI from showing the browser option when it won't actually work.

async checkWebGpu(): Promise<boolean> {
  if (!navigator.gpu) return false;
  try {
    const adapter = await navigator.gpu.requestAdapter();
    return adapter !== null;
  } catch {
    return false;
  }
}

If WebGPU is unavailable, the browser model option shows "WebGPU not available" and is grayed out.

2025-01-15 FEATURE

Local Model Support via WebGPU

Keating now runs entirely in your browser using WebGPU. Run Gemma 4 E4B locally without any API keys. The model loads progressively and caches in your browser for subsequent sessions.

Requires Chrome 113+ or Edge 113+ with WebGPU support. Model size: ~5GB cached locally.

2025-01-10 RELEASE

v0.1.0 - Initial Public Release

First public release of Keating hyperteacher. Built on the Pi agent framework with support for Google Gemini, Anthropic Claude, and OpenAI GPT models. Features Socratic teaching methodology with diagnostic-first approach.

  • - Chat-based teaching interface
  • - Multi-provider support
  • - Local session persistence
  • - Dark mode support
2025-01-05 DEV LOG

The Hyperteacher Philosophy

Keating is named after John Keating from Dead Poets Society, who taught that the purpose of education is not to fill minds but to ignite them. Our AI doesn't give answers - it forces you to reconstruct understanding from memory.

Core principle: struggle is the feature, not the bug. Neural pathways form through effort.

STAY_UPDATED

Follow development on GitHub or watch the repository for release notifications.

[BACK_TO_HOME]

import { useState } from "react"; import { Link } from "react-router-dom"; import { Nav } from "../components/Nav"; import { SimpleFooter } from "../components/Footer"; type TutorialTab = "browser" | "ollama" | "llamacpp" | "litellm" | "cloud"; const TABS: { id: TutorialTab; label: string }[] = [ { id: "browser", label: "[BROWSER]" }, { id: "ollama", label: "[OLLAMA]" }, { id: "llamacpp", label: "[LLAMA.CPP]" }, { id: "litellm", label: "[LITELLM]" }, { id: "cloud", label: "[CLOUD]" }, ]; export function Tutorial() { const [activeTab, setActiveTab] = useState("browser"); return (

Model Setup Guide

Choose how Keating runs AI models

{/* Model Types Overview */}

BROWSER ZERO SETUP

Runs entirely in your browser using WebGPU. No installation, no API keys, no server. Just open and chat.

  • - Uses Transformers.js + ONNX models
  • - Model cached in browser (~5GB)
  • - Works offline after first load
  • - Privacy: data never leaves device

LOCAL REQUIRES SETUP

Run any model locally with Ollama, llama.cpp, LiteLLM, or llmfit. More model choices, better performance.

  • - Use any GGUF model
  • - GPU acceleration (CUDA/Metal)
  • - No internet required
  • - Set endpoint in settings
{/* Detailed Tabs */}
{TABS.map((tab) => ( ))}
{/* Browser Tab */} {activeTab === "browser" && (

Browser WebGPU (Zero Setup)

The simplest option — just use Keating in a supported browser. No installation required.

# Requirements:

Chrome 113+ / Edge 113+ / Firefox Nightly (WebGPU flag)

GPU with WebGPU support (most modern GPUs)

~5GB free disk space for model cache

{[ "Open Keating web app in Chrome or Edge", 'Select "Gemma 4 E4B (Browser)" as model', "Wait for model to download and cache (~5GB, one-time)", "Chat! Works offline for future sessions", ].map((step, i) => (
0{i + 1}. {step}
))}

NO_API_KEY_REQUIRED

Your conversations never leave your device. Completely private.

)} {/* Ollama Tab */} {activeTab === "ollama" && (

Ollama

Popular local LLM runner with excellent GPU support. Works with any GGUF model.

# Install Ollama:

curl -fsSL https://ollama.com/install.sh | sh

# Pull a model:

ollama pull gemma3:4b

# Start server (runs on port 11434):

ollama serve

01. Install Ollama from{" "} ollama.com
02. Pull your preferred model:{" "} ollama pull gemma3:4b
03. In Keating settings, add custom provider:

Provider: ollama

Base URL: http://localhost:11434

Model: gemma3:4b (or your model name)

GPU_ACCELERATION

Ollama auto-detects CUDA (NVIDIA) and Metal (macOS). No API key needed for local.

)} {/* llama.cpp Tab */} {activeTab === "llamacpp" && (

llama.cpp

Lightweight C++ inference. Maximum control and performance. Runs any GGUF model.

# Clone and build:

git clone https://github.com/ggerganov/llama.cpp

cd llama.cpp && make

# Download a GGUF model:

wget https://huggingface.co/unsloth/gemma-4-E4B-it-GGUF/resolve/main/gemma-4-E4B-it-UD-Q4_K_XL.gguf

# Run server:

./llama-server -m gemma-4-E4B-it-UD-Q4_K_XL.gguf --port 8080

01. Build llama.cpp from{" "} GitHub
02. Download a GGUF model from HuggingFace
03. Start the server with your model
04. In Keating settings, add custom provider pointing to{" "} http://localhost:8080

TIP

Use{" "} -ngl 99 to offload all layers to GPU. Use{" "} -c 8192 for larger context.

)} {/* LiteLLM Tab */} {activeTab === "litellm" && (

LiteLLM

Unified API proxy that works with 100+ LLM providers. Exposes an OpenAI-compatible endpoint.

# Install:

pip install litellm

# Run with a local model:

litellm --model ollama/gemma3:4b

# Or with API keys (env vars):

export OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-...

export ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-ant-...

litellm --port 4000

01. Install:{" "} pip install litellm
02. Set API keys as environment variables (if using cloud providers)
03. Start proxy:{" "} litellm --port 4000
04. Point Keating to{" "} http://localhost:4000

UNIFIED_API

LiteLLM gives you one OpenAI-compatible endpoint that can route to any provider (local or cloud).

)} {/* Cloud Tab */} {activeTab === "cloud" && (

Cloud Providers

Use managed AI services for best performance and model variety. Requires API keys.

Google AI Studio (Gemini)

  1. 1. Go to{" "} aistudio.google.com/app/apikey
  2. 2. Sign in and click "Create API Key"
  3. 3. Copy key (starts with "AIza...")
  4. 4. Paste in Keating settings

Free tier: 15 req/min, 1M tokens/day

Synthetic

  1. 1. Create or copy your Synthetic API key
  2. 2. In Keating settings, add a custom provider named{" "} synthetic
  3. 3. Set the provider type to{" "} Synthetic (OpenAI Compatible)
  4. 4. Use{" "} https://api.synthetic.new/openai/v1 {" "} as the base URL

Use this when you want Synthetic's hosted models through an OpenAI-compatible endpoint.

Anthropic (Claude)

  1. 1. Go to{" "} console.anthropic.com
  2. 2. Create account and navigate to API Keys
  3. 3. Click "Create Key" and copy
  4. 4. Paste in Keating settings

Pricing: Claude Sonnet $3/M input, $15/M output

OpenAI (GPT)

  1. 1. Go to{" "} platform.openai.com/api-keys
  2. 2. Create account and click "Create new secret key"
  3. 3. Copy immediately (shown only once)
  4. 4. Paste in Keating settings

Pricing: GPT-4o $2.50/M input, $10/M output

)}
{/* Security Note */}

SECURITY_NOTE

API keys are stored locally in your browser's IndexedDB (web) or{" "} ~/.keating/.env (CLI). They never leave your device. Never commit keys to git.

); }
