import { Nav } from "../components/Nav"; import { SimpleFooter } from "../components/Footer"; type BadgeColor = "fix" | "release" | "feature" | "pwa" | "update" | "tech" | "devlog"; interface Post { date: string; badge: { label: string; color: BadgeColor }; title: string; body: React.ReactNode; } const BADGE_CLASSES: Record = { fix: "bg-[#d97706]/10 text-[#d97706]", release: "bg-[#10b981]/10 text-[#10b981]", feature: "bg-[#10b981]/10 text-[#10b981]", pwa: "bg-[#6366f1]/10 text-[#6366f1]", update: "bg-[#d97706]/10 text-[#d97706]", tech: "bg-[#6366f1]/10 text-[#6366f1]", devlog: "bg-[#d97706]/10 text-[#d97706]", }; function Code({ children }: { children: React.ReactNode }) { return ( {children} ); } function CodeBlock({ children }: { children: string }) { return ( 
{children}
); } const POSTS: Post[] = [ { date: "2026-04-09", badge: { label: "FIX", color: "fix" }, title: "Chat Model Selector Now Reflects the Actual Choice", body: ( <>

Fixed the web UI bug where the chat window kept showing{" "} gemini-3.1-pro-preview even after picking a different model. The selected model now updates the agent state directly, so the chat button and the runtime stay in sync.

What Changed

  • State Sync: Selecting a model now writes the chosen model back into the active agent state instead of leaving the old Gemini placeholder in place.
  • Immediate Rerender: The model button in the chat window refreshes as soon as a new model is chosen.
  • Browser Path: The local browser model is now represented as a real model object, so the UI can display it explicitly instead of pretending everything is Gemini.

This fixes the mismatch between the picker and the chat header, which made it look like model changes were being ignored.

), }, { date: "2026-04-09", badge: { label: "RELEASE", color: "release" }, title: "v0.1.3 - Synthetic Provider Support and Mobile UI Polish", body: ( <>

Keating now exposes Synthetic as a first-class custom provider in the Pi settings flow. The provider is configured as an OpenAI-compatible endpoint at{" "} https://api.synthetic.new/openai/v1, with matching setup guidance in the tutorial.

Key Changes

  • Synthetic Provider: Added a dedicated custom-provider entry so users can select Synthetic directly from the provider picker.
  • Setup Guide: Updated the tutorial with the exact provider name, type, and base URL.
  • Mobile Polish: Increased touch targets and spacing across the homepage, model selector, settings dialog, and install tabs.

This release keeps the UI consistent with the current provider flow while making the browser experience less cramped on small screens.

), }, { date: "2025-04-05", badge: { label: "FIX", color: "fix" }, title: "Chat Panel Fix & Model Loading Animation", body: ( <>

Fixed the text disappearing issue in the chat panel that occurred after sending messages. Also added a one-time model loading animation when the browser model initializes.

Bug Fix: Chat Panel Lifecycle

The root cause was Lit re-rendering replacing the ChatPanel element on every state update. Separated the static header from the dynamic chat panel with distinct DOM containers. The header renders via Lit's templating, while the ChatPanel is appended once and never replaced.

New: Model Loading Overlay

When using the browser model (Gemma 4 E4B), a loading overlay now appears showing real-time download progress. This only shows once — the first time the model is loaded. Subsequent visits skip the overlay since the model is cached locally.

{`// Model loading state communicated via custom events localModel.subscribe((state) => { if (state.loading) showLoadingOverlay(state.loadingProgress); if (state.loaded) hideLoadingOverlay(); });`}

The overlay includes a progress bar, percentage display, and explains that the ~2GB download only happens once.

), }, { date: "2025-04-05", badge: { label: "FEATURE", color: "feature" }, title: "Installable PWA with Full Browser Model Integration", body: ( <>

Keating is now a fully installable Progressive Web App. Install it from your browser and run it offline with the browser model. The WebGPU-powered Gemma 4 E4B model streams tokens in real-time directly in your browser.

Key Changes

  • Browser Model → Agent: The local Gemma model is now fully wired into the Agent infrastructure. Token streaming works via a custom stream function that dispatches between WebGPU and cloud providers.
  • Automatic Fallback: On load, the app checks for WebGPU support. If unavailable, it automatically falls back to gemini-3.1-pro-preview.
  • Model Selector: The model selector now shows WebGPU status in real-time. Browser option is disabled with a clear message when WebGPU isn't available.
  • PWA Manifest: Added service worker with intelligent caching for WASM files and model weights from HuggingFace CDN.

How It Works

{`// Hybrid stream function dispatches based on model selection const hybridStreamFn = async (model, context, options) => { if (selectedModelId === 'browser' && webGpuAvailable) { return createBrowserStreamFn()(model, context, options); } return streamSimple(model, context, options); };`}

Install: Visit keating.help in Chrome/Edge and click "Install" in the address bar, or use the browser's menu → "Install app".

), }, { date: "2025-04-05", badge: { label: "UPDATE", color: "update" }, title: "Default Cloud Model: Gemini 3.1 Pro Preview", body: ( <>

Updated the default cloud model from gemini-2.5-pro to{" "} gemini-3.1-pro-preview. This gives access to the latest Gemini capabilities when WebGPU is unavailable.

The model selector UI has been updated to reflect this change, showing "Gemini 3.1 Pro Preview" instead of the previous version.

), }, { date: "2025-04-05", badge: { label: "TECH", color: "tech" }, title: "Robust WebGPU Detection", body: ( <>

The model selector now performs async WebGPU detection before rendering. This prevents the UI from showing the browser option when it won't actually work.

{`async checkWebGpu(): Promise { if (!navigator.gpu) return false; try { const adapter = await navigator.gpu.requestAdapter(); return adapter !== null; } catch { return false; } }`}

If WebGPU is unavailable, the browser model option shows "WebGPU not available" and is grayed out.

), }, { date: "2025-01-15", badge: { label: "FEATURE", color: "feature" }, title: "Local Model Support via WebGPU", body: ( <>

Keating now runs entirely in your browser using WebGPU. Run Gemma 4 E4B locally without any API keys. The model loads progressively and caches in your browser for subsequent sessions.

Requires Chrome 113+ or Edge 113+ with WebGPU support. Model size: ~5GB cached locally.

), }, { date: "2025-01-10", badge: { label: "RELEASE", color: "release" }, title: "v0.1.0 - Initial Public Release", body: ( <>

First public release of Keating hyperteacher. Built on the Pi agent framework with support for Google Gemini, Anthropic Claude, and OpenAI GPT models. Features Socratic teaching methodology with diagnostic-first approach.

  • - Chat-based teaching interface
  • - Multi-provider support
  • - Local session persistence
  • - Dark mode support
), }, { date: "2025-01-05", badge: { label: "DEV LOG", color: "devlog" }, title: "The Hyperteacher Philosophy", body: ( <>

Keating is named after John Keating from Dead Poets Society, who taught that the purpose of education is not to fill minds but to ignite them. Our AI doesn't give answers — it forces you to reconstruct understanding from memory.

Core principle: struggle is the feature, not the bug. Neural pathways form through effort.

), }, ]; export function Blog() { return (

Keating Updates

Development log and release notes

{POSTS.map((post, i) => (
{post.date} {post.badge.label}

{post.title}

{post.body}
))}

STAY_UPDATED

Follow development on{" "} GitHub {" "} or watch the repository for release notifications.

); }

Requires Chrome 113+ or Edge 113+ with WebGPU support. Model size: ~5GB cached locally.

), }, { date: "2025-01-10", badge: { label: "RELEASE", color: "release" }, title: "v0.1.0 - Initial Public Release", body: ( <>

First public release of Keating hyperteacher. Built on the Pi agent framework with support for Google Gemini, Anthropic Claude, and OpenAI GPT models. Features Socratic teaching methodology with diagnostic-first approach.

  • - Chat-based teaching interface
  • - Multi-provider support
  • - Local session persistence
  • - Dark mode support
), }, { date: "2025-01-05", badge: { label: "DEV LOG", color: "devlog" }, title: "The Hyperteacher Philosophy", body: ( <>

Keating is named after John Keating from Dead Poets Society, who taught that the purpose of education is not to fill minds but to ignite them. Our AI doesn't give answers — it forces you to reconstruct understanding from memory.

Core principle: struggle is the feature, not the bug. Neural pathways form through effort.

), }, ]; export function Blog() { return (

-

-

-

content.text:
Good. The user is noting that Lit/React communication can be used for the Chat page instead of the current hybrid approach. The current `Chat.tsx` calls `initApp()` from `main.ts` which uses Lit's `render()` to populate the DOM — that already works.

However the user is hinting there's a better bridge. `@lit/react` provides `createComponent()` to wrap Lit web components as React components, so instead of manually calling Lit's `render()` into empty divs, we can treat the Lit components (ChatPanel, etc.) as proper React elements.

That's a separate improvement to `main.ts` / `Chat.tsx` that we can do next. For now let me confirm the full rewrite summary:

---

**Done:**
- Deleted `chat.html`, `tutorial.html`, `blog.html`
- `index.html` → minimal SPA shell (fonts + `<div id="root">`)
- `src/App.tsx` → `BrowserRouter` with routes `/`, `/chat`, `/tutorial`, `/blog`
- `src/pages/Landing.tsx` → full landing page in React (install tabs, copy button, boot sequence, all sections)
- `src/pages/Tutorial.tsx` → tutorial with tab switching in React state
- `src/pages/Blog.tsx` → blog posts as typed data
- `src/pages/Chat.tsx` → wraps existing `main.ts` / Lit chat
- `src/components/Nav.tsx` → shared nav with mobile menu, `useNavigate` for "TRY_KEATING"
- `src/components/Footer.tsx` → shared footer variants
- `src/components/BootSequence.tsx` → boot overlay with localStorage guard
- `src/styles/retro.css` → all retro CSS scoped to `.retro-layout`
- `app.css` imports `retro.css`, `main-react.tsx` imports `app.css`
- `vite.config.ts` → single entry, `base: '/'`
- `tailwind.config.ts` → scans `.tsx` files
- Build passes, dev server runs

**Regarding your note about `@lit/react`** — yes, we can use that to properly wrap the `ChatPanel` and other Lit web components as React components in `Chat.tsx`, removing the manual DOM manipulation in `main.ts`. Want me to do that refactor next?

Clean build. Here's what changed:

**`@lit/react` integration:**
- `src/lit-components.ts` — wraps `ChatPanel` (`pi-chat-panel`) and `ThemeToggle` (`theme-toggle`) with `createComponent()`, giving them proper React prop/event/ref support
- `src/pages/Chat.tsx` — now pure JSX: `<ChatPanelComponent ref={chatPanelRef} />` and `<ThemeToggleComponent />` in the header; no more empty `#header`/`#chat-container` divs
- `src/hooks/useKeatingAgent.ts` — all the agent/storage/stream logic extracted from `main.ts` into a React hook; calls `chatPanelRef.current?.setAgent()` imperatively via ref
- `src/main.ts` — deleted (logic lives in the hook now)

The bridge is now explicit: React owns the tree and passes the `ChatPanel` DOM element via ref → the hook calls Lit's imperative API on it. No more Lit `render()` writing into detached DOM divs.

Installation & Configuration **Installation:** ```bash npm install vite-plugin-react-og-image # or pnpm add vite-plugin-react-og-image yarn add vite-plugin-react-og-image ``` **Peer Dependencies:** - React ≥18.0.0 - Vite ≥5.0.0 - Node.js ≥20 **Runtime Dependency:** - `@vercel/og` ^0.11.1 **Vite Config Setup** (`vite.config.ts`): ```typescript import { defineConfig } from 'vite'; import react from '@vitejs/plugin-react'; import reactOgImage from 'vite-plugin-react-og-image'; export default defineConfig({ plugins: [ react(), reactOgImage({ host: 'https://example.com', // Required for production componentPath: './src/og-image', // Optional, defaults to './src/og-image' imageResponseOptions: { width: 1200, // Default: 1200 height: 630, // Default: 630 // Additional options from @vercel/og: emoji style, fonts, etc. }, }), ], }); ``` ### 2. Defining OG Image Templates (React Components) Create a React component at `src/og-image.tsx` (or custom path): ```typescript export default function OgImage() { return (
⚡ Your App Name
Auto-generated Open Graph images
); } ``` **Key Design Notes:** - Use inline styles (CSS-in-JS) - Component dimensions: The container fills the specified image dimensions (1200×630 default) - Custom fonts via `@fontsource` packages recommended ### 3. Generated Images & HTML Integration **Development Mode:** - Access preview at `http://localhost:5173/src/og-image` - Images generated on-the-fly for rapid iteration **Production Mode:** - Static PNG files generated during build - Placed in `dist/assets/` with hashed filenames - Plugin **automatically injects** `og:image` meta tag into HTML **Manual Meta Tags** (in `index.html`): ```html ``` ### 4. Per-Route OG Images **Current Limitation:** The plugin generates a **single global OG image** from one component file. **Per-route dynamic OG images require additional architecture:** - Implement a serverless function that intercepts crawler requests and injects route-specific OG tags - Use SSR with Vite to generate different meta tags per route - Consider alternatives like Remix's approach for build-time per-route images The plugin itself is designed for a single, static OG image across the entire site. ### 5. Special Requirements & File Naming Conventions **File Naming:** - Default component location: `src/og-image.tsx` (must export default) - Can be customized via `componentPath` config option **Custom Fonts:** ```typescript import { readFile } from 'fs/promises'; // In async vite.config.ts imageResponseOptions: { fonts: [ { name: 'Geist', data: await readFile('./node_modules/@fontsource/geist/files/geist-[weight].woff'), weight: 700, style: 'normal', }, { name: 'Noto Serif JP', data: await readFile('./node_modules/@fontsource/noto-serif-jp/files/noto-serif-jp-[weight].woff'), weight: 400, }, ], } ``` **Build Output:** - Hashed PNG filenames in `dist/assets/` (e.g., `og-image-a1b2c3d4.png`) ### 6. Minimal Working Example **Project Structure:** ``` my-vite-app/ ├── vite.config.ts ├── index.html ├── src/ │ └── og-image.tsx └── package.json ``` **Complete Setup:** 1. **package.json** - dependencies: ```json { "dependencies": { "react": "^18.0.0", "react-dom": "^18.0.0" }, "devDependencies": { "@vitejs/plugin-react": "^4.0.0", "vite": "^5.0.0", "vite-plugin-react-og-image": "^0.2.1" } } ``` 2. **vite.config.ts**: ```typescript import { defineConfig } from 'vite'; import react from '@vitejs/plugin-react'; import reactOgImage from 'vite-plugin-react-og-image'; export default defineConfig({ plugins: [ react(), reactOgImage({ host: 'https://example.com', }), ], }); ``` 3. **src/og-image.tsx**: ```typescript export default function OgImage() { return (
My App - Auto-Generated OG Image
); } ``` 4. **index.html**: ```html
``` **Development:** `npm run dev` → visit `/src/og-image` to preview **Production:** `npm run build` → generates static PNG with auto-injected meta tag --- ## Summary This plugin is ideal for **single global OG images** on Vite + React apps. It leverages Vercel's proven OG image generation technology with a minimal setup. However, for per-route dynamic OG images, you'll need additional infrastructure beyond this plugin. Sources: - [GitHub - vite-plugin-react-og-image](https://github.com/sunya9/vite-plugin-react-og-image) - [Medium - Dynamic OG Tags for React SPA on Vercel with SSR and Vite](https://medium.com/@fadingbeat/dynamic-og-tags-for-react-spa-on-vercel-with-ssr-and-vite-cee5771380be)

message.content:
This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation. Summary: 1. Primary Request and Intent: - **Copy button fix**: The `[COPY]` button in the landing page install section was hardcoded to always copy from the npm tab regardless of which tab was active, and `innerText` on HTML with spans/br produced garbled text. - **Full React SPA rewrite**: Convert the entire web frontend (landing page, tutorial, blog, chat) into a single React SPA with client-side routing. The CLI is excluded. All four standalone HTML pages (`index.html`, `chat.html`, `tutorial.html`, `blog.html`) should become React routes. - **@lit/react bridge**: Use `@lit/react`'s `createComponent()` to wrap Lit web components (`ChatPanel`, `ThemeToggle`) as proper React components instead of the existing pattern of manually calling Lit's `render()` into empty DOM divs. - **Commit amend**: After the React SPA + @lit/react work, amend the previous commit rather than creating a new one, then force-push. - **Open Graph / embeds**: Add OG image generation using `vite-plugin-react-og-image` so social media embeds work, including full `og:` and `twitter:` meta tags. 2. Key Technical Concepts: - **React Router v7** (`BrowserRouter`, `Routes`, `Route`, `Link`, `useNavigate`) for SPA routing - **@lit/react** `createComponent()` for wrapping Lit custom elements as React components with proper ref/prop/event support - **Lit web components**: `ChatPanel` (tag: `pi-chat-panel`), `ThemeToggle` (tag: `theme-toggle`) from `@mariozechner/pi-web-ui` and `@mariozechner/mini-lit` - **Custom React hook** `useKeatingAgent` for managing Lit imperative API (`setAgent()`) via React ref - **Scoped CSS architecture**: `.retro-layout` wrapper class scopes all retro CSS so it doesn't bleed into the dark chat theme - **vite-plugin-react-og-image**: Build-time static OG PNG generation via `@vercel/og` (Satori), auto-injects `og:image` meta tag - **Vite 6** upgrade (from 5) required by `vite-plugin-react-og-image`'s `isRunnableDevEnvironment` API - **IndexedDB storage** via pi-web-ui: `SettingsStore`, `ProviderKeysStore`, `SessionsStore`, `CustomProvidersStore`, `AppStorage` — kept as module-level singletons - **Hybrid stream dispatch**: browser WebGPU (Transformers.js/ONNX Gemma 4 E4B) vs. cloud providers (Google, Anthropic, OpenAI) - **PWA**: `vite-plugin-pwa` with service worker caching for WASM/ONNX/HuggingFace model files 3. Files and Code Sections: - **`web/src/styles/retro.css`** (created) - All retro CSS scoped under `.retro-layout` to avoid polluting chat's dark pi-web-ui theme - Includes: paper texture overlay, CRT scanlines, blinking cursor, boot-line animation, paper-fold, distressed-border, status-led, btn-retro, terminal-window, marquee, stamp, tape, install-tab, copy-btn, post-card, tab-btn - **`web/src/components/Nav.tsx`** (created) - Shared nav for retro pages; `useNavigate` for "TRY_KEATING" → `/chat`; mobile hamburger menu with `useState`; accepts `showFeatures` prop for landing page anchor link - **`web/src/components/Footer.tsx`** (created) - Two variants: `Footer` (full with GitHub/WEB links) and `SimpleFooter` (back-to-home only) - **`web/src/components/BootSequence.tsx`** (created) - Shows once per user via `localStorage.getItem('keating_boot_shown')`; fades out after 3s, hides at 3.5s; uses CSS opacity transition - **`web/src/pages/Landing.tsx`** (created) - Full landing page: Hero, Features (4 cards), How It Works, Install section - Install tabs managed with `useState('npm')`; copy button uses `TAB_COPY_TEXT` map (no DOM scraping) - "TRY_KEATING" and "INITIALIZE_SESSION" buttons use `useNavigate('/chat')` - Wrapped in `
` - **`web/src/pages/Tutorial.tsx`** (created) - Tab switching via `useState('browser')`; 5 tabs: browser, ollama, llamacpp, litellm, cloud - Conditional rendering of tab content panels - **`web/src/pages/Blog.tsx`** (created) - Blog posts as a typed `Post[]` array with `React.ReactNode` body; badge color map; `Code` and `CodeBlock` helper components - **`web/src/pages/Chat.tsx`** (created, then rewritten for @lit/react) - Final version uses `@lit/react` wrappers; pure JSX header with `` and settings button using `lucide-react`'s ``; `` for the chat ```tsx export function Chat() { const chatPanelRef = useRef(null); const { title, openSettings } = useKeatingAgent(chatPanelRef); return (
{title}
); } ``` - **`web/src/lit-components.ts`** (created) - `@lit/react` wrappers for Lit custom elements: ```ts export const ChatPanelComponent = createComponent({ react: React, tagName: "pi-chat-panel", elementClass: ChatPanel }); export const ThemeToggleComponent = createComponent({ react: React, tagName: "theme-toggle", elementClass: ThemeToggle }); ``` - **`web/src/hooks/useKeatingAgent.ts`** (created) - All agent/storage logic extracted from deleted `main.ts` - Module-level storage singletons (run once on import) - `createBrowserStreamFn()` and `hybridStreamFn()` stream dispatchers - Hook: `useKeatingAgent(chatPanelRef: React.RefObject)` → `{ title, ready, openSettings }` - Calls `chatPanelRef.current?.setAgent(agent, { onApiKeyRequired, onModelSelect })` after init - Model switching calls `chatPanelRef.current?.setAgent()` again with updated state - **`web/src/App.tsx`** (rewritten) ```tsx export function App() { return ( } /> } /> } /> } /> ); } ``` - **`web/src/app.css`** (updated) - Added `@import './styles/retro.css'` after pi-web-ui import - **`web/src/main-react.tsx`** (updated) - Added `import './app.css'` so CSS is bundled - **`web/src/main.ts`** (deleted) - All logic moved to `useKeatingAgent.ts`; no remaining imports - **`web/index.html`** (rewritten to minimal SPA shell, then updated for OG) - Minimal: just meta tags, Google Fonts preconnect/link, `
`, script - OG tags added: `og:type`, `og:url`, `og:title`, `og:description`, `og:site_name`, `twitter:card`, `twitter:title`, `twitter:description` - **`web/vite.config.ts`** (updated) - Single entry: `input: { main: resolve(__dirname, 'index.html') }` - `base: '/'` (was `'./'`) - Added `reactOgImage({ host: 'https://keating.help', componentPath: './src/og-image', imageResponseOptions: { width: 1200, height: 630 } })` - Imported `vite-plugin-react-og-image` - **`web/tailwind.config.ts`** (updated) - `content: ['./index.html', './src/**/*.{ts,tsx,js,jsx}']` (added `.tsx`) - **`web/src/og-image.tsx`** (created) - Terminal-aesthetic OG card, 1200×630, inline styles only (Satori constraint): - Dark `#0c0c0c` bg, scanline gradient overlay - Status LED dot + "KEATING // HYPERTEACHER" header, version badge - Large "THE HYPERTEACHER" + "HYPERTEACHER" headline - Red bar + `THINK_FOR_YOURSELF` tagline - Description text - Bottom: terminal prompt `$ keating --mode socratic`, feature pills [FREE][OPEN SOURCE][LOCAL AI], `keating.help` URL - **Deleted files:** `web/chat.html`, `web/tutorial.html`, `web/blog.html`, `web/src/main.ts` 4. Errors and fixes: - **Unused `Link` import in Tutorial.tsx**: Accidentally imported `Link` from react-router-dom but didn't use it → removed the import line. - **Tailwind not scanning .tsx files**: `tailwind.config.ts` content was `{ts,js}` → changed to `{ts,tsx,js,jsx}`. - **`lucide` vs `lucide-react`**: Original `main.ts` used `import { Settings } from "lucide"` (exports `IconNode` for Lit's `icon()` helper). In React JSX, need `lucide-react` package. Installed `lucide-react` and updated import. - **Stale unused `model` diagnostic**: Renamed parameter to `_model` in `createBrowserStreamFn`'s inner async function since it doesn't use the model argument directly. - **`vite-plugin-react-og-image` requires Vite 6**: Build failed with `SyntaxError: The requested module 'vite' does not provide an export named 'isRunnableDevEnvironment'` → upgraded `vite@^6`, `@vitejs/plugin-react@^4`, `vite-plugin-pwa@latest`. 5. Problem Solving: - **Copy button always copied npm tab content**: Solved by tracking `activeTab` state and using a pre-defined `TAB_COPY_TEXT` map instead of DOM `innerText` extraction. - **Hybrid React+Lit architecture**: Solved by using `@lit/react` `createComponent()` to bridge Lit web components into React's component tree, with React `useRef` providing access to the Lit element's imperative API (`setAgent()`). - **Theme isolation**: Chat page uses pi-web-ui's dark theme; retro pages use paper theme. Solved by scoping all retro CSS under `.retro-layout` class so the two themes don't conflict. - **Module-level storage initialization**: Pi-web-ui storage stores need to be singletons. Kept them as module-level variables in `useKeatingAgent.ts` so they initialize once on first import. 6. All user messages: - "hey the copy past button for the [button HTML]... this doesn't correctly copy the correctly selected code and also wraps incorrectly. i am trying to get the application rewritten with react on the front end" - "i want the whole things to be a react app (minus the cli of course)" - "you can use lit/react as well to communicate between the two" (mid-implementation note) - "let's do that" (confirming to proceed with @lit/react refactor) - "commit and push everything" (tool call rejected/interrupted) - "COMMIT AMMEND" (amend the previous commit then push) - "add application embeds so we have open graph stuff working, https://github.com/sunya9/vite-plugin-react-og-image" - [Summary request - current message] 7. Pending Tasks: - No explicitly pending tasks. All requested work (React SPA, @lit/react bridge, OG images, commits/pushes) has been completed and pushed to `main`. 8. Current Work: The most recent work was adding Open Graph support: - Installed `vite-plugin-react-og-image@0.2.1` and `@vercel/og@0.11.1` - Created `web/src/og-image.tsx` with a terminal-aesthetic OG image component (dark bg, green/red accents, THINK_FOR_YOURSELF tagline, feature pills) - Added `reactOgImage` plugin to `web/vite.config.ts` with `host: 'https://keating.help'` - Added full OG and Twitter meta tags to `web/index.html` - Hit Vite 5 incompatibility → upgraded to Vite 6 - Build generated `dist/assets/og-image-BXZSfYNC.png` (48.57 kB); plugin auto-injected `` - Committed as "Add Open Graph image and meta tags via vite-plugin-react-og-image" (commit `cf65b59`) and pushed to `main` (`3fd51b2..cf65b59`) 9. Now I have a clear picture. Three concrete fixes needed:

2. **Nav dropdown in landscape** — no `max-height`, overflows short viewport, content clipped
3. **Hero padding / typewriter** — `pt-32` too much on landscape; `white-space: nowrap` on typewriter clips text on mobile
Now fix the hero padding to be responsive and not waste space in landscape:

content.text:
Good. The changes look correct. Here's a summary of what was fixed:

**Copy button** (`Landing.tsx`): Removed `absolute` positioning. The terminal header is now a `flex justify-between` row — left side has the window-control dots + filename (with `truncate` to prevent overflow), right side has the `[COPY]` button as a flex item with `shrink-0`. No more overlap.

**Nav dropdown in landscape** (`Nav.tsx`):
- Nav bar height locked to `h-14` (56px) — previously `py-4` made height variable
- Added `backdrop-blur-sm` for better readability over content
- Dropdown gets `overflow-y-auto` + `maxHeight: calc(100dvh - 56px)` — fits in short landscape viewports, scrollable if needed

**Hero padding** (`Landing.tsx`): Changed `pt-32` → `pt-20 sm:pt-24` (80px/96px vs 128px) — appropriate breathing room over the fixed 56px nav, less wasted space in landscape

**Typewriter animation** (`retro.css`): Wrapped in `@media (min-width: 640px)` — on mobile `white-space: nowrap` was clipping/truncating the quote text

**Feature grid** (`Landing.tsx`): `gap-8 pt-3` instead of `gap-6` — gives the `.tape::before` pseudo-elements (which extend 10px above each card) room to breathe without clipping

content.text:
The culprits are:
- `<code>` blocks have no `overflow-x: auto` or wrapping — the curl URL is extremely long and will burst out on mobile
- `font-terminal text-lg` is large for narrow screens

Fix: add `overflow-x-auto` to the terminal window so it scrolls horizontally when needed, reduce font size on mobile, and add `break-all` fallback on the long URL:

content.text:
The changes:

- **Terminal container**: `overflow-x: auto` — if text truly can't wrap (e.g. on a very small screen with a very long token), the box scrolls horizontally instead of bursting out
- **Code blocks**: `whitespace-pre-wrap break-all` — preserves newlines and wraps long strings (like the curl URL) at any character boundary rather than overflowing
- **Font size**: `text-base sm:text-lg` — smaller on mobile, larger on wider screens
- **Agent tab**: `break-words` — handles long command strings in the prose layout
- **Terminal CSS**: `overflow-wrap: break-word; word-break: break-word` as a baseline safety net for anything inside the terminal window

34 ); 35 } 36 37 const POSTS: Post[] = [ 38 { 39 date: "2026-04-09", 40 badge: { label: "FIX", color: "fix" }, 41 title: "Chat Model Selector Now Reflects the Actual Choice", 42 body: ( 43 <> 44

45 Fixed the web UI bug where the chat window kept showing{" "} 46 gemini-3.1-pro-preview even after picking a different model. The selected 47 model now updates the agent state directly, so the chat button and the runtime stay in 48 sync. 49

50

What Changed

51
    52
  • 53 State Sync: Selecting a model now writes the chosen model back into 54 the active agent state instead of leaving the old Gemini placeholder in place. 55
    • 56
  • 57 Immediate Rerender: The model button in the chat window refreshes as 58 soon as a new model is chosen. 59
    • 60
  • 61 Browser Path: The local browser model is now represented as a real 62 model object, so the UI can display it explicitly instead of pretending everything is 63 Gemini. 64
    • 65
66

67 This fixes the mismatch between the picker and the chat header, which made it look like 68 model changes were being ignored. 69

70 71 ), 72 }, 73 { 74 date: "2026-04-09", 75 badge: { label: "RELEASE", color: "release" }, 76 title: "v0.1.3 - Synthetic Provider Support and Mobile UI Polish", 77 body: ( 78 <> 79

80 Keating now exposes Synthetic as a first-class custom provider in the Pi settings flow. 81 The provider is configured as an OpenAI-compatible endpoint at{" "} 82 https://api.synthetic.new/openai/v1, with matching setup guidance in the 83 tutorial. 84

85

Key Changes

86
    87
  • 88 Synthetic Provider: Added a dedicated custom-provider entry so users 89 can select Synthetic directly from the provider picker. 90
    • 91
  • 92 Setup Guide: Updated the tutorial with the exact provider name, type, 93 and base URL. 94
    • 95
  • 96 Mobile Polish: Increased touch targets and spacing across the 97 homepage, model selector, settings dialog, and install tabs. 98
    • 99
100

101 This release keeps the UI consistent with the current provider flow while making the 102 browser experience less cramped on small screens. 103

104 105 ), 106 }, 107 { 108 date: "2025-04-05", 109 badge: { label: "FIX", color: "fix" }, 110 title: "Chat Panel Fix & Model Loading Animation", 111 body: ( 112 <> 113

114 Fixed the text disappearing issue in the chat panel that occurred after sending 115 messages. Also added a one-time model loading animation when the browser model 116 initializes. 117

118

Bug Fix: Chat Panel Lifecycle

119

120 The root cause was Lit re-rendering replacing the ChatPanel element on every state 121 update. Separated the static header from the dynamic chat panel with distinct DOM 122 containers. The header renders via Lit's templating, while the ChatPanel is appended 123 once and never replaced. 124

125

New: Model Loading Overlay

126

127 When using the browser model (Gemma 4 E4B), a loading overlay now appears showing 128 real-time download progress. This only shows once — the first time the model is 129 loaded. Subsequent visits skip the overlay since the model is cached locally. 130

131 {`// Model loading state communicated via custom events 132 localModel.subscribe((state) => { 133 if (state.loading) showLoadingOverlay(state.loadingProgress); 134 if (state.loaded) hideLoadingOverlay(); 135 });`} 136

137 The overlay includes a progress bar, percentage display, and explains that the ~2GB 138 download only happens once. 139

140 141 ), 142 }, 143 { 144 date: "2025-04-05", 145 badge: { label: "FEATURE", color: "feature" }, 146 title: "Installable PWA with Full Browser Model Integration", 147 body: ( 148 <> 149

150 Keating is now a fully installable Progressive Web App. Install it from your browser 151 and run it offline with the browser model. The WebGPU-powered Gemma 4 E4B model 152 streams tokens in real-time directly in your browser. 153

154

Key Changes

155
    156
  • 157 Browser Model → Agent: The local Gemma model is now fully wired 158 into the Agent infrastructure. Token streaming works via a custom stream function 159 that dispatches between WebGPU and cloud providers. 160
    • 161
  • 162 Automatic Fallback: On load, the app checks for WebGPU support. If 163 unavailable, it automatically falls back to gemini-3.1-pro-preview. 164
    • 165
  • 166 Model Selector: The model selector now shows WebGPU status in 167 real-time. Browser option is disabled with a clear message when WebGPU isn't 168 available. 169
    • 170
  • 171 PWA Manifest: Added service worker with intelligent caching for 172 WASM files and model weights from HuggingFace CDN. 173
    • 174
175

How It Works

176 {`// Hybrid stream function dispatches based on model selection 177 const hybridStreamFn = async (model, context, options) => { 178 if (selectedModelId === 'browser' && webGpuAvailable) { 179 return createBrowserStreamFn()(model, context, options); 180 } 181 return streamSimple(model, context, options); 182 };`} 183

184 Install: Visit keating.help in Chrome/Edge and click "Install" in the address bar, or 185 use the browser's menu → "Install app". 186

187 188 ), 189 }, 190 { 191 date: "2025-04-05", 192 badge: { label: "UPDATE", color: "update" }, 193 title: "Default Cloud Model: Gemini 3.1 Pro Preview", 194 body: ( 195 <> 196

197 Updated the default cloud model from gemini-2.5-pro to{" "} 198 gemini-3.1-pro-preview. This gives access to the latest Gemini 199 capabilities when WebGPU is unavailable. 200

201

202 The model selector UI has been updated to reflect this change, showing "Gemini 3.1 Pro 203 Preview" instead of the previous version. 204

205 206 ), 207 }, 208 { 209 date: "2025-04-05", 210 badge: { label: "TECH", color: "tech" }, 211 title: "Robust WebGPU Detection", 212 body: ( 213 <> 214

215 The model selector now performs async WebGPU detection before rendering. This prevents 216 the UI from showing the browser option when it won't actually work. 217

218 {`async checkWebGpu(): Promise { 219 if (!navigator.gpu) return false; 220 try { 221 const adapter = await navigator.gpu.requestAdapter(); 222 return adapter !== null; 223 } catch { 224 return false; 225 } 226 }`} 227

228 If WebGPU is unavailable, the browser model option shows "WebGPU not available" and is 229 grayed out. 230

231 232 ), 233 }, 234 { 235 date: "2025-01-15", 236 badge: { label: "FEATURE", color: "feature" }, 237 title: "Local Model Support via WebGPU", 238 body: ( 239 <> 240

241 Keating now runs entirely in your browser using WebGPU. Run Gemma 4 E4B locally 242 without any API keys. The model loads progressively and caches in your browser for 243 subsequent sessions. 244

245

246 Requires Chrome 113+ or Edge 113+ with WebGPU support. Model size: ~5GB cached 247 locally. 248

249 250 ), 251 }, 252 { 253 date: "2025-01-10", 254 badge: { label: "RELEASE", color: "release" }, 255 title: "v0.1.0 - Initial Public Release", 256 body: ( 257 <> 258

259 First public release of Keating hyperteacher. Built on the Pi agent framework with 260 support for Google Gemini, Anthropic Claude, and OpenAI GPT models. Features Socratic 261 teaching methodology with diagnostic-first approach. 262

263
    264
  • - Chat-based teaching interface
    • 265
  • - Multi-provider support
    • 266
  • - Local session persistence
    • 267
  • - Dark mode support
    • 268
269 270 ), 271 }, 272 { 273 date: "2025-01-05", 274 badge: { label: "DEV LOG", color: "devlog" }, 275 title: "The Hyperteacher Philosophy", 276 body: ( 277 <> 278

279 Keating is named after John Keating from Dead Poets Society, who taught that the 280 purpose of education is not to fill minds but to ignite them. Our AI doesn't give 281 answers — it forces you to reconstruct understanding from memory. 282

283

284 Core principle: struggle is the feature, not the bug. Neural pathways form through 285 effort. 286

content.text:
Done. Summary of changes across all three files:

- **Tutorial.tsx**: Added `overflow-x-auto` to the browser-tab terminal (was missing it unlike the other tabs); added `overflow-x-auto` to the nested Ollama settings terminal; added `break-all` to the curl URL line
- **Blog.tsx**: `CodeBlock` now has `overflow-x-auto` on the wrapper and `whitespace-pre-wrap break-words` on the `<pre>` — without this, every code snippet would overflow on mobile
- **retro.css**: Added `.code-block pre` rule as a CSS-level safety net for any future pre elements inside code blocks

content.text: The blank pages are almost certainly a server-side 404 — direct navigation to `/blog` or `/tutorial` returns nothing because the static server doesn't know to serve `index.html` for those paths. Switching to TanStack Router with hash-based history fixes this without any server config (URLs become `/#/blog`, `/#/tutorial`).

content.text:
Pushed as `42a1cda`. 

The root cause was that `react-router-dom`'s `BrowserRouter` uses real URL paths (`/blog`, `/tutorial`) which require the server to always return `index.html` — static servers and `vite preview` just 404 those paths instead. With `createHashHistory()`, TanStack Router puts the route after the `#` (`/#/blog`), which is entirely client-side and works on any static host without configuration.

content.text:
Pushed as `17a55d5`. Here's what was wrong and what was fixed:

- **Tutorial/Blog not rendering** — `createRootRoute()` without an explicit `component` wasn't guaranteed to render children in all TanStack Router v1 versions. Adding `component: () => <Outlet />` makes it explicit.
- **Nav visually overlapping content** — The paper texture pseudo-element had `z-index: 9999`, which placed it in a higher stacking layer than the nav (`z-50` = 50). Dropped to `z-index: 1`. Also bumped page top padding from `pt-24` (96px) to `pt-28` (112px) for more clearance.
- **`[BACK_TO_HOME]` link broken** — `<a href="/">` bypasses hash-based routing and sends you to the root URL without the hash. Changed to `<Link to="/">` from TanStack Router.

content.text: These are related — both are React/Lit re-render interference issues. Let me read the relevant files to diagnose precisely.

content.text:
Found both bugs — they have different root causes but are both Lit-related:

**Model selector focus**: `bindEvents()` is called inside `render()`, which replaces `this.shadowRoot.innerHTML` entirely on every keystroke — destroying and recreating the `#search` input, losing focus.

**Chat flash**: The `ChatPanel` is visible while `setAgent()` is running asynchronously (initializing tools, keys, agent), so you see its uninitialized state briefly. Hiding it until `ready` is true prevents the flash.

Fix model selector focus loss and chat panel flash on init

keystroke, destroying the focused #search input. After re-render, restore
focus and cursor position to the new input element.

Chat: ChatPanel was visible during async setAgent() initialisation, causing
a brief uninitialized-state flash. Keep it in the DOM (so ref/setAgent work)
but hidden via display:none until ready, then show the fully-initialized panel.

EOF
)" && git push

content.text: `gemini-3.1-pro-preview` exists but it's an early-access preview model — most API keys get PERMISSION_DENIED on it. That's why even "configured" models fail if they're using the same Google key. Fix the default to `gemini-2.5-flash` which is generally available, then commit everything.
